<pr_description>
Improve logging when using PartialObjectMetadata
This week, we spent some time debugging a complex issue in cert-manager approver-policy, and we would have been a lot more effective if controller-runtime had logged information about the actual resource (probably kind) behind the `PartialObjectMetadata` when the controller only cares for metadata of resources. Below is a redacted extract of the logs from a real cluster having this issue. We would love to know which resource cache was not synced in time, since approver-policy is watching for metadata on multiple resources. 😅

    36	var (
    37	        _ Informers     = &informerCache{}
    38	        _ client.Reader = &informerCache{}
    39	        _ Cache         = &informerCache{}
    40	)
    41	
    42	// ErrCacheNotStarted is returned when trying to read from the cache that wasn't started.
    43	type ErrCacheNotStarted struct{}
    44	
    45	func (*ErrCacheNotStarted) Error() string {
    46	        return "the cache is not started, can not read objects"
    47	}
    48	
    49	var _ error = (*ErrCacheNotStarted)(nil)
    50	
    51	// ErrResourceNotCached indicates that the resource type
    52	// the client asked the cache for is not cached, i.e. the
    53	// corresponding informer does not exist yet.
    54	type ErrResourceNotCached = internal.ErrResourceNotCached
    55	
    56	// informerCache is a Kubernetes Object cache populated from internal.Informers.
    57	// informerCache wraps internal.Informers.
    58	type informerCache struct {
    59	        scheme *runtime.Scheme
    60	        *internal.Informers
    61	        readerFailOnMissingInformer bool
    62	}
    63	
    64	// Get implements Reader.
    65	func (ic *informerCache) Get(ctx context.Context, key client.ObjectKey, out client.Object, opts ...client.GetOption) error {
    66	        gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(out, ic.scheme)
    67	        if err != nil {
    68	                return err
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        started, cache, err := ic.getInformerForKind(ctx, gvk, out)
    72	        if err != nil {
    73	                return err
    74	        }
    75	
    76	        if !started {
    77	                return &ErrCacheNotStarted{}
    78	        }
    79	        return cache.Reader.Get(ctx, key, out, opts...)
    80	}
    81	
    82	// List implements Reader.
    83	func (ic *informerCache) List(ctx context.Context, out client.ObjectList, opts ...client.ListOption) error {
    84	        gvk, cacheTypeObj, err := ic.objectTypeForListObject(out)
    85	        if err != nil {
    86	                return err
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        started, cache, err := ic.getInformerForKind(ctx, *gvk, cacheTypeObj)
    90	        if err != nil {
    91	                return err
    92	        }
    93	
    94	        if !started {
    95	                return &ErrCacheNotStarted{}
    96	        }
    97	
    98	        return cache.Reader.List(ctx, out, opts...)
    99	}
   100	
   101	// objectTypeForListObject tries to find the runtime.Object and associated GVK
   102	// for a single object corresponding to the passed-in list type. We need them
   103	// because they are used as cache map key.
   104	func (ic *informerCache) objectTypeForListObject(list client.ObjectList) (*schema.GroupVersionKind, runtime.Object, error) {
   105	        gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(list, ic.scheme)
   106	        if err != nil {
   107	                return nil, nil, err
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        // We need the non-list GVK, so chop off the "List" from the end of the kind.
   111	        gvk.Kind = strings.TrimSuffix(gvk.Kind, "List")
   112	
   113	        // Handle unstructured.UnstructuredList.
   114	        if _, isUnstructured := list.(runtime.Unstructured); isUnstructured {
   115	                u := &unstructured.Unstructured{}
   116	                u.SetGroupVersionKind(gvk)
   117	                return &gvk, u, nil
   118	        }
   119	        // Handle metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList.
   120	        if _, isPartialObjectMetadata := list.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); isPartialObjectMetadata {
   121	                pom := &metav1.PartialObjectMetadata{}
   122	                pom.SetGroupVersionKind(gvk)
   123	                return &gvk, pom, nil
   124	        }
   125	
   126	        // Any other list type should have a corresponding non-list type registered
   127	        // in the scheme. Use that to create a new instance of the non-list type.
   128	        cacheTypeObj, err := ic.scheme.New(gvk)
   129	        if err != nil {
   130	                return nil, nil, err
   131	        }
   132	        return &gvk, cacheTypeObj, nil
   133	}
   134	
   135	func applyGetOptions(opts ...InformerGetOption) *internal.GetOptions {
   136	        cfg := &InformerGetOptions{}
   137	        for _, opt := range opts {
   138	                opt(cfg)
   139	        }
   140	        return (*internal.GetOptions)(cfg)
   141	}
   142	
   143	// GetInformerForKind returns the informer for the GroupVersionKind. If no informer exists, one will be started.
   144	func (ic *informerCache) GetInformerForKind(ctx context.Context, gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, opts ...InformerGetOption) (Informer, error) {
   145	        // Map the gvk to an object
   146	        obj, err := ic.scheme.New(gvk)
   147	        if err != nil {
   148	                return nil, err
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        _, i, err := ic.Informers.Get(ctx, gvk, obj, false, applyGetOptions(opts...))
   152	        if err != nil {
   153	                return nil, err
   154	        }
   155	        return i.Informer, nil
   156	}
   157	
   158	// GetInformer returns the informer for the obj. If no informer exists, one will be started.
   159	func (ic *informerCache) GetInformer(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object, opts ...InformerGetOption) (Informer, error) {
   160	        gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(obj, ic.scheme)
   161	        if err != nil {
   162	                return nil, err
   163	        }
   164	
   165	        _, i, err := ic.Informers.Get(ctx, gvk, obj, false, applyGetOptions(opts...))
   166	        if err != nil {
   167	                return nil, err
   168	        }
   169	        return i.Informer, nil
   170	}
   171	
   172	func (ic *informerCache) getInformerForKind(ctx context.Context, gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object) (bool, *internal.Cache, error) {
   173	        started, cache, err := ic.Informers.Get(ctx, gvk, obj, ic.readerFailOnMissingInformer, &internal.GetOptions{})
   174	        if err != nil {
   175	                return false, nil, err
   176	        }
   177	        return started, cache, nil
   178	}
   179	
   180	// RemoveInformer deactivates and removes the informer from the cache.
   181	func (ic *informerCache) RemoveInformer(_ context.Context, obj client.Object) error {
   182	        gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(obj, ic.scheme)
   183	        if err != nil {
   184	                return err
   185	        }
   186	
   187	        ic.Informers.Remove(gvk, obj)
   188	        return nil
   189	}
   190	
   191	// NeedLeaderElection implements the LeaderElectionRunnable interface
   192	// to indicate that this can be started without requiring the leader lock.
   193	func (ic *informerCache) NeedLeaderElection() bool {
   194	        return false
   195	}
   196	
   197	// IndexField adds an indexer to the underlying informer, using extractValue function to get
   198	// value(s) from the given field. This index can then be used by passing a field selector
   199	// to List. For one-to-one compatibility with "normal" field selectors, only return one value.
   200	// The values may be anything. They will automatically be prefixed with the namespace of the
   201	// given object, if present. The objects passed are guaranteed to be objects of the correct type.
   202	func (ic *informerCache) IndexField(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object, field string, extractValue client.IndexerFunc) error {
   203	        informer, err := ic.GetInformer(ctx, obj, BlockUntilSynced(false))
   204	        if err != nil {
   205	                return err
   206	        }
   207	        return indexByField(informer, field, extractValue)
   208	}
   209	
   210	func indexByField(informer Informer, field string, extractValue client.IndexerFunc) error {
   211	        indexFunc := func(objRaw any) ([]string, error) {
   212	                // TODO(directxman12): check if this is the correct type?
   213	                obj, isObj := objRaw.(client.Object)
   214	                if !isObj {
   215	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("object of type %T is not an Object", objRaw)
   216	                }
   217	                meta, err := apimeta.Accessor(obj)
   218	                if err != nil {
   219	                        return nil, err
   220	                }
   221	                ns := meta.GetNamespace()
   222	
   223	                rawVals := extractValue(obj)
   224	                var vals []string
   225	                if ns == "" {
   226	                        // if we're not doubling the keys for the namespaced case, just create a new slice with same length
   227	                        vals = make([]string, len(rawVals))
   228	                } else {
   229	                        // if we need to add non-namespaced versions too, double the length
   230	                        vals = make([]string, len(rawVals)*2)
   231	                }
   232	                for i, rawVal := range rawVals {
   233	                        // save a namespaced variant, so that we can ask
   234	                        // "what are all the object matching a given index *in a given namespace*"
   235	                        vals[i] = internal.KeyToNamespacedKey(ns, rawVal)
   236	                        if ns != "" {
   237	                                // if we have a namespace, also inject a special index key for listing
   238	                                // regardless of the object namespace
   239	                                vals[i+len(rawVals)] = internal.KeyToNamespacedKey("", rawVal)
   240	                        }
   241	                }
   242	
   243	                return vals, nil
   244	        }
   245	
   246	        return informer.AddIndexers(cache.Indexers{internal.FieldIndexName(field): indexFunc})
   247	}
   248

    39	        "k8s.io/client-go/rest"

    46	var log = logf.RuntimeLog.WithName("cache")
    47	
    48	// ErrResourceNotCached indicates that the resource type
    49	// the client asked the cache for is not cached, i.e. the
    50	// corresponding informer does not exist yet.
    51	type ErrResourceNotCached struct {
    52	        GVK schema.GroupVersionKind
    53	}
    54	
    55	// Error returns the error
    56	func (r ErrResourceNotCached) Error() string {
    57	        return fmt.Sprintf("%s is not cached", r.GVK.String())
    58	}
    59	
    60	var _ error = (*ErrResourceNotCached)(nil)
    61	
    62	// InformersOpts configures an InformerMap.
    63	type InformersOpts struct {
    64	        HTTPClient            *http.Client
    65	        Scheme                *runtime.Scheme
    66	        Mapper                meta.RESTMapper
    67	        ResyncPeriod          time.Duration
    68	        Namespace             string
    69	        NewInformer           func(cache.ListerWatcher, runtime.Object, time.Duration, cache.Indexers) cache.SharedIndexInformer
    70	        Selector              Selector
    71	        Transform             cache.TransformFunc
    72	        UnsafeDisableDeepCopy bool
    73	        EnableWatchBookmarks  bool
    74	        WatchErrorHandler     cache.WatchErrorHandlerWithContext
    75	}
    76	
    77	// NewInformers creates a new InformersMap that can create informers under the hood.
    78	func NewInformers(config *rest.Config, options *InformersOpts) *Informers {
    79	        newInformer := cache.NewSharedIndexInformer
    80	        if options.NewInformer != nil {
    81	                newInformer = options.NewInformer
    82	        }
    83	        return &Informers{
    84	                config:     config,
    85	                httpClient: options.HTTPClient,
    86	                scheme:     options.Scheme,
    87	                mapper:     options.Mapper,
    88	                tracker: tracker{
    89	                        Structured:   make(map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache),
    90	                        Unstructured: make(map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache),
    91	                        Metadata:     make(map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache),
    92	                },
    93	                codecs:                serializer.NewCodecFactory(options.Scheme),
    94	                paramCodec:            runtime.NewParameterCodec(options.Scheme),
    95	                resync:                options.ResyncPeriod,
    96	                startWait:             make(chan struct{}),
    97	                namespace:             options.Namespace,
    98	                selector:              options.Selector,
    99	                transform:             options.Transform,
   100	                unsafeDisableDeepCopy: options.UnsafeDisableDeepCopy,
   101	                enableWatchBookmarks:  options.EnableWatchBookmarks,
   102	                newInformer:           newInformer,
   103	                watchErrorHandler:     options.WatchErrorHandler,
   104	        }
   105	}
   106	
   107	// Cache contains the cached data for an Cache.
   108	type Cache struct {
   109	        // Informer is the cached informer
   110	        Informer cache.SharedIndexInformer
   111	
   112	        // CacheReader wraps Informer and implements the CacheReader interface for a single type
   113	        Reader CacheReader
   114	
   115	        // Stop can be used to stop this individual informer.
   116	        stop chan struct{}
   117	}
   118	
   119	// Start starts the informer managed by a MapEntry.
   120	// Blocks until the informer stops. The informer can be stopped
   121	// either individually (via the entry's stop channel) or globally
   122	// via the provided stop argument.
   123	func (c *Cache) Start(stop <-chan struct{}) {
   124	        // Stop on either the whole map stopping or just this informer being removed.
   125	        internalStop, cancel := syncs.MergeChans(stop, c.stop)
   126	        defer cancel()
   127	        // Convert the stop channel to a context and then add the logger.
   128	        c.Informer.RunWithContext(logr.NewContext(wait.ContextForChannel(internalStop), log))
   129	}
   130	
   131	type tracker struct {
   132	        Structured   map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache
   133	        Unstructured map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache
   134	        Metadata     map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache
   135	}
   136	
   137	// GetOptions provides configuration to customize the behavior when
   138	// getting an informer.
   139	type GetOptions struct {
   140	        // BlockUntilSynced controls if the informer retrieval will block until the informer is synced. Defaults to `true`.
   141	        BlockUntilSynced *bool
   142	}
   143	
   144	// Informers create and caches Informers for (runtime.Object, schema.GroupVersionKind) pairs.
   145	// It uses a standard parameter codec constructed based on the given generated Scheme.
   146	type Informers struct {
   147	        // httpClient is used to create a new REST client
   148	        httpClient *http.Client
   149	
   150	        // scheme maps runtime.Objects to GroupVersionKinds
   151	        scheme *runtime.Scheme
   152	
   153	        // config is used to talk to the apiserver
   154	        config *rest.Config
   155	
   156	        // mapper maps GroupVersionKinds to Resources
   157	        mapper meta.RESTMapper
   158	
   159	        // tracker tracks informers keyed by their type and groupVersionKind
   160	        tracker tracker
   161	
   162	        // codecs is used to create a new REST client
   163	        codecs serializer.CodecFactory
   164	
   165	        // paramCodec is used by list and watch
   166	        paramCodec runtime.ParameterCodec
   167	
   168	        // resync is the base frequency the informers are resynced
   169	        // a 10 percent jitter will be added to the resync period between informers
   170	        // so that all informers will not send list requests simultaneously.
   171	        resync time.Duration
   172	
   173	        // mu guards access to the map
   174	        mu sync.RWMutex
   175	
   176	        // started is true if the informers have been started
   177	        started bool
   178	
   179	        // startWait is a channel that is closed after the
   180	        // informer has been started.
   181	        startWait chan struct{}
   182	
   183	        // waitGroup is the wait group that is used to wait for all informers to stop
   184	        waitGroup sync.WaitGroup
   185	
   186	        // stopped is true if the informers have been stopped
   187	        stopped bool
   188	
   189	        // ctx is the context to stop informers
   190	        ctx context.Context
   191	
   192	        // namespace is the namespace that all ListWatches are restricted to
   193	        // default or empty string means all namespaces
   194	        namespace string
   195	
   196	        selector              Selector
   197	        transform             cache.TransformFunc
   198	        unsafeDisableDeepCopy bool
   199	        enableWatchBookmarks  bool
   200	
   201	        // NewInformer allows overriding of the shared index informer constructor for testing.
   202	        newInformer func(cache.ListerWatcher, runtime.Object, time.Duration, cache.Indexers) cache.SharedIndexInformer
   203	
   204	        // watchErrorHandler allows the shared index informer's
   205	        // watchErrorHandler to be set by overriding the options
   206	        // or to use the default watchErrorHandler
   207	        watchErrorHandler cache.WatchErrorHandlerWithContext
   208	}
   209	
   210	// Start calls Run on each of the informers and sets started to true. Blocks on the context.
   211	// It doesn't return start because it can't return an error, and it's not a runnable directly.
   212	func (ip *Informers) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   213	        if err := func() error {
   214	                ip.mu.Lock()
   215	                defer ip.mu.Unlock()
   216	
   217	                if ip.started {
   218	                        return errors.New("informer already started") //nolint:stylecheck
   219	                }
   220	
   221	                // Set the context so it can be passed to informers that are added later
   222	                ip.ctx = ctx
   223	
   224	                // Start each informer
   225	                for _, i := range ip.tracker.Structured {
   226	                        ip.startInformerLocked(i)
   227	                }
   228	                for _, i := range ip.tracker.Unstructured {
   229	                        ip.startInformerLocked(i)
   230	                }
   231	                for _, i := range ip.tracker.Metadata {
   232	                        ip.startInformerLocked(i)
   233	                }
   234	
   235	                // Set started to true so we immediately start any informers added later.
   236	                ip.started = true
   237	                close(ip.startWait)
   238	
   239	                return nil
   240	        }(); err != nil {
   241	                return err
   242	        }
   243	        <-ctx.Done() // Block until the context is done
   244	        ip.mu.Lock()
   245	        ip.stopped = true // Set stopped to true so we don't start any new informers
   246	        ip.mu.Unlock()
   247	        ip.waitGroup.Wait() // Block until all informers have stopped
   248	        return nil
   249	}
   250	
   251	func (ip *Informers) startInformerLocked(cacheEntry *Cache) {
   252	        // Don't start the informer in case we are already waiting for the items in
   253	        // the waitGroup to finish, since waitGroups don't support waiting and adding
   254	        // at the same time.
   255	        if ip.stopped {
   256	                return
   257	        }
   258	
   259	        ip.waitGroup.Go(func() {
   260	                cacheEntry.Start(ip.ctx.Done())
   261	        })
   262	}
   263	
   264	func (ip *Informers) waitForStarted(ctx context.Context) bool {
   265	        select {
   266	        case <-ip.startWait:
   267	                return true
   268	        case <-ctx.Done():
   269	                return false
   270	        }
   271	}
   272	
   273	// getHasSyncedFuncs returns all the HasSynced functions for the informers in this map.
   274	func (ip *Informers) getHasSyncedFuncs() []cache.InformerSynced {
   275	        ip.mu.RLock()
   276	        defer ip.mu.RUnlock()
   277	
   278	        res := make([]cache.InformerSynced, 0,
   279	                len(ip.tracker.Structured)+len(ip.tracker.Unstructured)+len(ip.tracker.Metadata),
   280	        )
   281	        for _, i := range ip.tracker.Structured {
   282	                res = append(res, i.Informer.HasSynced)
   283	        }
   284	        for _, i := range ip.tracker.Unstructured {
   285	                res = append(res, i.Informer.HasSynced)
   286	        }
   287	        for _, i := range ip.tracker.Metadata {
   288	                res = append(res, i.Informer.HasSynced)
   289	        }
   290	        return res
   291	}
   292	
   293	// WaitForCacheSync waits until all the caches have been started and synced.
   294	func (ip *Informers) WaitForCacheSync(ctx context.Context) bool {
   295	        if !ip.waitForStarted(ctx) {
   296	                return false
   297	        }
   298	        return cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), ip.getHasSyncedFuncs()...)
   299	}
   300	
   301	// Peek attempts to get the informer for the GVK, but does not start one if one does not exist.
   302	func (ip *Informers) Peek(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object) (res *Cache, started bool, ok bool) {
   303	        ip.mu.RLock()
   304	        defer ip.mu.RUnlock()
   305	        i, ok := ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk]
   306	        return i, ip.started, ok
   307	}
   308	
   309	// Get will create a new Informer and add it to the map of specificInformersMap if none exists. Returns
   310	// the Informer from the map.
   311	func (ip *Informers) Get(ctx context.Context, gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object, readerFailOnMissingInformer bool, opts *GetOptions) (bool, *Cache, error) {
   312	        // Return the informer if it is found
   313	        i, started, ok := ip.Peek(gvk, obj)
   314	        if !ok {
   315	                if readerFailOnMissingInformer {
   316	                        return false, nil, &ErrResourceNotCached{GVK: gvk}
   317	                }
   318	                var err error
   319	                if i, started, err = ip.addInformerToMap(gvk, obj); err != nil {
   320	                        return started, nil, err
   321	                }
   322	        }
   323	
   324	        shouldBlock := true
   325	        if opts.BlockUntilSynced != nil {
   326	                shouldBlock = *opts.BlockUntilSynced
   327	        }
   328	
   329	        if shouldBlock && started && !i.Informer.HasSynced() {
   330	                // Wait for it to sync before returning the Informer so that folks don't read from a stale cache.
   331	                if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), i.Informer.HasSynced) {
   332	                        return started, nil, apierrors.NewTimeoutError(fmt.Sprintf("failed waiting for %T Informer to sync", obj), 0)
   333	                }
   334	        }
   335	
   336	        return started, i, nil
   337	}
   338	
   339	// Remove removes an informer entry and stops it if it was running.
   340	func (ip *Informers) Remove(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object) {
   341	        ip.mu.Lock()
   342	        defer ip.mu.Unlock()
   343	
   344	        informerMap := ip.informersByType(obj)
   345	
   346	        entry, ok := informerMap[gvk]
   347	        if !ok {
   348	                return
   349	        }
   350	        close(entry.stop)
   351	        delete(informerMap, gvk)
   352	}
   353	
   354	func (ip *Informers) informersByType(obj runtime.Object) map[schema.GroupVersionKind]*Cache {
   355	        switch obj.(type) {
   356	        case runtime.Unstructured:
   357	                return ip.tracker.Unstructured
   358	        case *metav1.PartialObjectMetadata, *metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList:
   359	                return ip.tracker.Metadata
   360	        default:
   361	                return ip.tracker.Structured
   362	        }
   363	}
   364	
   365	// addInformerToMap either returns an existing informer or creates a new informer, adds it to the map and returns it.
   366	func (ip *Informers) addInformerToMap(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object) (*Cache, bool, error) {
   367	        ip.mu.Lock()
   368	        defer ip.mu.Unlock()
   369	
   370	        // Check the cache to see if we already have an Informer. If we do, return the Informer.
   371	        // This is for the case where 2 routines tried to get the informer when it wasn't in the map
   372	        // so neither returned early, but the first one created it.
   373	        if i, ok := ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk]; ok {
   374	                return i, ip.started, nil
   375	        }
   376	
   377	        // Create a NewSharedIndexInformer and add it to the map.
   378	        listWatcher, err := ip.makeListWatcher(gvk, obj)
   379	        if err != nil {
   380	                return nil, false, err
   381	        }
   382	        sharedIndexInformer := ip.newInformer(&cache.ListWatch{
   383	                ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
   384	                        ip.selector.ApplyToList(&opts)
   385	                        return listWatcher.ListWithContextFunc(ctx, opts)
   386	                },
   387	                WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
   388	                        opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
   389	                        opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
   390	
   391	                        ip.selector.ApplyToList(&opts)
   392	                        return listWatcher.WatchFuncWithContext(ctx, opts)
   393	                },
   394	        }, obj, calculateResyncPeriod(ip.resync), cache.Indexers{
   395	                cache.NamespaceIndex: cache.MetaNamespaceIndexFunc,
   396	        })
   397	
   398	        // Set WatchErrorHandler on SharedIndexInformer if set
   399	        if ip.watchErrorHandler != nil {
   400	                if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext(ip.watchErrorHandler); err != nil {
   401	                        return nil, false, err
   402	                }
   403	        }
   404	
   405	        // Check to see if there is a transformer for this gvk
   406	        if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetTransform(ip.transform); err != nil {
   407	                return nil, false, err
   408	        }
   409	
   410	        mapping, err := ip.mapper.RESTMapping(gvk.GroupKind(), gvk.Version)
   411	        if err != nil {
   412	                return nil, false, err
   413	        }
   414	
   415	        // Create the new entry and set it in the map.
   416	        i := &Cache{
   417	                Informer: sharedIndexInformer,
   418	                Reader: CacheReader{
   419	                        indexer:          sharedIndexInformer.GetIndexer(),
   420	                        groupVersionKind: gvk,
   421	                        scopeName:        mapping.Scope.Name(),
   422	                        disableDeepCopy:  ip.unsafeDisableDeepCopy,
   423	                },
   424	                stop: make(chan struct{}),
   425	        }
   426	        ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk] = i
   427	
   428	        // Start the informer in case the InformersMap has started, otherwise it will be
   429	        // started when the InformersMap starts.
   430	        if ip.started {
   431	                ip.startInformerLocked(i)
   432	        }
   433	        return i, ip.started, nil
   434	}
   435	
   436	func (ip *Informers) makeListWatcher(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, obj runtime.Object) (*cache.ListWatch, error) {
   437	        // Kubernetes APIs work against Resources, not GroupVersionKinds.  Map the
   438	        // groupVersionKind to the Resource API we will use.
   439	        mapping, err := ip.mapper.RESTMapping(gvk.GroupKind(), gvk.Version)
   440	        if err != nil {
   441	                return nil, err
   442	        }
   443	
   444	        // Figure out if the GVK we're dealing with is global, or namespace scoped.
   445	        var namespace string
   446	        if mapping.Scope.Name() == meta.RESTScopeNameNamespace {
   447	                namespace = restrictNamespaceBySelector(ip.namespace, ip.selector)
   448	        }
   449	
   450	        switch obj.(type) {
   451	        //
   452	        // Unstructured
   453	        //
   454	        case runtime.Unstructured:
   455	                // If the rest configuration has a negotiated serializer passed in,
   456	                // we should remove it and use the one that the dynamic client sets for us.
   457	                cfg := rest.CopyConfig(ip.config)
   458	                cfg.NegotiatedSerializer = nil
   459	                dynamicClient, err := dynamic.NewForConfigAndClient(cfg, ip.httpClient)
   460	                if err != nil {
   461	                        return nil, err
   462	                }
   463	                resources := dynamicClient.Resource(mapping.Resource)
   464	                return &cache.ListWatch{
   465	                        ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
   466	                                if namespace != "" {
   467	                                        return resources.Namespace(namespace).List(ctx, opts)
   468	                                }
   469	                                return resources.List(ctx, opts)
   470	                        },
   471	                        // Setup the watch function
   472	                        WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
   473	                                opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
   474	                                opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
   475	
   476	                                if namespace != "" {
   477	                                        return resources.Namespace(namespace).Watch(ctx, opts)
   478	                                }
   479	                                return resources.Watch(ctx, opts)
   480	                        },
   481	                }, nil
   482	        //
   483	        // Metadata
   484	        //
   485	        case *metav1.PartialObjectMetadata, *metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList:
   486	                // Always clear the negotiated serializer and use the one
   487	                // set from the metadata client.
   488	                cfg := rest.CopyConfig(ip.config)
   489	                cfg.NegotiatedSerializer = nil
   490	
   491	                // Grab the metadata metadataClient.
   492	                metadataCli<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

     1	package internal
     2	
     3	import (
     4	        "context"
     5	        "errors"
     6	        "fmt"
     7	        "reflect"
     8	        "time"
     9	
    10	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
    11	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    12	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    13	        toolscache "k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
    14	        "k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue"
    15	        logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/internal/log"
    16	
    17	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache"
    18	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
    19	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler"
    20	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate"
    21	)
    22	
    23	var logKind = logf.RuntimeLog.WithName("source").WithName("Kind")
    24	
    25	// Kind is used to provide a source of events originating inside the cluster from Watches (e.g. Pod Create).
    26	type Kind[object client.Object, request comparable] struct {
    27	        // Type is the type of object to watch.  e.g. &v1.Pod{}
    28	        Type object
    29	
    30	        // Cache used to watch APIs
    31	        Cache cache.Cache
    32	
    33	        Handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request]
    34	
    35	        Predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
    36	
    37	        // startedErr may contain an error if one was encountered during startup. If its closed and does not
    38	        // contain an error, startup and syncing finished.
    39	        startedErr  chan error
    40	        startCancel func()
    41	}
    42	
    43	// Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to register an EventHandler with the Informer
    44	// to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
    45	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error {
    46	        if isNil(ks.Type) {
    47	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil object")
    48	        }
    49	        if isNil(ks.Cache) {
    50	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil cache")
    51	        }
    52	        if isNil(ks.Handler) {
    53	                return errors.New("must create Kind with non-nil handler")
    54	        }
    55	
    56	        // cache.GetInformer will block until its context is cancelled if the cache was already started and it can not
    57	        // sync that informer (most commonly due to RBAC issues).
    58	        ctx, ks.startCancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
    59	        ks.startedErr = make(chan error, 1) // Buffer chan to not leak goroutines if WaitForSync isn't called
    60	        go func() {
    61	                var (
    62	                        i       cache.Informer
    63	                        lastErr error
    64	                )
    65	
    66	                // Tries to get an informer until it returns true,
    67	                // an error or the specified context is cancelled or expired.
    68	                if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
    69	                        // Lookup the Informer from the Cache and add an EventHandler which populates the Queue
    70	                        i, lastErr = ks.Cache.GetInformer(ctx, ks.Type)
    71	                        if lastErr != nil {
    72	                                kindMatchErr := &meta.NoKindMatchError{}
    73	                                switch {
    74	                                case errors.As(lastErr, &kindMatchErr):
    75	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start",
    76	                                                "kind", kindMatchErr.GroupKind)
    77	                                case runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(lastErr):
    78	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "kind must be registered to the Scheme")
    79	                                default:
    80	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "failed to get informer from cache")
    81	                                }
    82	                                return false, nil // Retry.
    83	                        }
    84	                        return true, nil
    85	                }); err != nil {
    86	                        if lastErr != nil {
    87	                                ks.startedErr <- fmt.Errorf("failed to get informer from cache: %w", lastErr)
    88	                                return
    89	                        }
    90	                        ks.startedErr <- err
    91	                        return
    92	                }
    93	
    94	                handlerRegistration, err := i.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, ks.Handler, ks.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
    95	                        Logger: &logKind,
    96	                })
    97	                if err != nil {
    98	                        ks.startedErr <- err
    99	                        return
   100	                }
   101	                // First, wait for the cache to sync. For real caches this waits for startup.
   102	                // For fakes with Synced=false, this returns immediately allowing fast failure.
   103	                if !ks.Cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx) {
   104	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("cache did not sync")
   105	                        close(ks.startedErr)
   106	                        return
   107	                }
   108	                // Then wait for this specific handler to receive all initial events.
   109	                if !toolscache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), handlerRegistration.HasSynced) {
   110	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("handler did not sync")
   111	                }
   112	                close(ks.startedErr)
   113	        }()
   114	
   115	        return nil
   116	}
   117	
   118	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
   119	        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
   120	                return fmt.Sprintf("kind source: %T", ks.Type)
   121	        }
   122	        return "kind source: unknown type"
   123	}
   124	
   125	// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
   126	// workers until the cache is synced.
   127	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
   128	        select {
   129	        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
   130	                return err
   131	        case <-ctx.Done():
   132	                ks.startCancel()
   133	                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
   134	                        return nil
   135	                }
   136	                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %T", ks.Type)
   137	        }
   138	}
   139	
   140	func isNil(arg any) bool {
   141	        if v := reflect.ValueOf(arg); !v.IsValid() || ((v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr ||
   142	                v.Kind() == reflect.Interface ||
   143	                v.Kind() == reflect.Slice ||
   144	                v.Kind() == reflect.Map ||
   145	                v.Kind() == reflect.Chan ||
   146	                v.Kind() == reflect.Func) && v.IsNil()) {
   147	                return true
   148	        }
   149	        return false
   150	}
   151

    41	// errReconciliationTimeout is the error used as the cause when the ReconciliationTimeout guardrail fires.
    42	// This allows us to distinguish wrapper timeouts from user-initiated context cancellations.
    43	var errReconciliationTimeout = errors.New("reconciliation timeout")
    44	
    45	// Options are the arguments for creating a new Controller.
    46	type Options[request comparable] struct {
    47	        // Reconciler is a function that can be called at any time with the Name / Namespace of an object and
    48	        // ensures that the state of the system matches the state specified in the object.
    49	        // Defaults to the DefaultReconcileFunc.
    50	        Do reconcile.TypedReconciler[request]
    51	
    52	        // RateLimiter is used to limit how frequently requests may be queued into the work queue.
    53	        RateLimiter workqueue.TypedRateLimiter[request]
    54	
    55	        // NewQueue constructs the queue for this controller once the controller is ready to start.
    56	        // This is a func because the standard Kubernetes work queues start themselves immediately, which
    57	        // leads to goroutine leaks if something calls controller.New repeatedly.
    58	        NewQueue func(controllerName string, rateLimiter workqueue.TypedRateLimiter[request]) workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]
    59	
    60	        // MaxConcurrentReconciles is the maximum number of concurrent Reconciles which can be run. Defaults to 1.
    61	        MaxConcurrentReconciles int
    62	
    63	        // CacheSyncTimeout refers to the time limit set on waiting for cache to sync
    64	        // Defaults to 2 minutes if not set.
    65	        CacheSyncTimeout time.Duration
    66	
    67	        // Name is used to uniquely identify a Controller in tracing, logging and monitoring.  Name is required.
    68	        Name string
    69	
    70	        // LogConstructor is used to construct a logger to then log messages to users during reconciliation,
    71	        // or for example when a watch is started.
    72	        // Note: LogConstructor has to be able to handle nil requests as we are also using it
    73	        // outside the context of a reconciliation.
    74	        LogConstructor func(request *request) logr.Logger
    75	
    76	        // RecoverPanic indicates whether the panic caused by reconcile should be recovered.
    77	        // Defaults to true.
    78	        RecoverPanic *bool
    79	
    80	        // LeaderElected indicates whether the controller is leader elected or always running.
    81	        LeaderElected *bool
    82	
    83	        // EnableWarmup specifies whether the controller should start its sources
    84	        // when the manager is not the leader.
    85	        // Defaults to false, which means that the controller will wait for leader election to start
    86	        // before starting sources.
    87	        EnableWarmup *bool
    88	
    89	        // ReconciliationTimeout is used as the timeout passed to the context of each Reconcile call.
    90	        // By default, there is no timeout.
    91	        ReconciliationTimeout time.Duration
    92	}
    93	
    94	// Controller implements controller.Controller.
    95	type Controller[request comparable] struct {
    96	        // Name is used to uniquely identify a Controller in tracing, logging and monitoring.  Name is required.
    97	        Name string
    98	
    99	        // MaxConcurrentReconciles is the maximum number of concurrent Reconciles which can be run. Defaults to 1.
   100	        MaxConcurrentReconciles int
   101	
   102	        // Reconciler is a function that can be called at any time with the Name / Namespace of an object and
   103	        // ensures that the state of the system matches the state specified in the object.
   104	        // Defaults to the DefaultReconcileFunc.
   105	        Do reconcile.TypedReconciler[request]
   106	
   107	        // RateLimiter is used to limit how frequently requests may be queued into the work queue.
   108	        RateLimiter workqueue.TypedRateLimiter[request]
   109	
   110	        // NewQueue constructs the queue for this controller once the controller is ready to start.
   111	        // This is a func because the standard Kubernetes work queues start themselves immediately, which
   112	        // leads to goroutine leaks if something calls controller.New repeatedly.
   113	        NewQueue func(controllerName string, rateLimiter workqueue.TypedRateLimiter[request]) workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]
   114	
   115	        // Queue is an listeningQueue that listens for events from Informers and adds object keys to
   116	        // the Queue for processing
   117	        Queue priorityqueue.PriorityQueue[request]
   118	
   119	        // mu is used to synchronize Controller setup
   120	        mu sync.Mutex
   121	
   122	        // Started is true if the Controller has been Started
   123	        Started bool
   124	
   125	        // ctx is the context that was passed to Start() and used when starting watches.
   126	        //
   127	        // According to the docs, contexts should not be stored in a struct: https://golang.org/pkg/context,
   128	        // while we usually always strive to follow best practices, we consider this a legacy case and it should
   129	        // undergo a major refactoring and redesign to allow for context to not be stored in a struct.
   130	        ctx context.Context
   131	
   132	        // CacheSyncTimeout refers to the time limit set on waiting for cache to sync
   133	        // Defaults to 2 minutes if not set.
   134	        CacheSyncTimeout time.Duration
   135	
   136	        // startWatches maintains a list of sources, handlers, and predicates to start when the controller is started.
   137	        startWatches []source.TypedSource[request]
   138	
   139	        // startedEventSourcesAndQueue is used to track if the event sources have been started.
   140	        // It ensures that we append sources to c.startWatches only until we call Start() / Warmup()
   141	        // It is true if startEventSourcesAndQueueLocked has been called at least once.
   142	        startedEventSourcesAndQueue bool
   143	
   144	        // didStartEventSourcesOnce is used to ensure that the event sources are only started once.
   145	        didStartEventSourcesOnce sync.Once
   146	
   147	        // LogConstructor is used to construct a logger to then log messages to users during reconciliation,
   148	        // or for example when a watch is started.
   149	        // Note: LogConstructor has to be able to handle nil requests as we are also using it
   150	        // outside the context of a reconciliation.
   151	        LogConstructor func(request *request) logr.Logger
   152	
   153	        // RecoverPanic indicates whether the panic caused by reconcile should be recovered.
   154	        // Defaults to true.
   155	        RecoverPanic *bool
   156	
   157	        // LeaderElected indicates whether the controller is leader elected or always running.
   158	        LeaderElected *bool
   159	
   160	        // EnableWarmup specifies whether the controller should start its sources when the manager is not
   161	        // the leader. This is useful for cases where sources take a long time to start, as it allows
   162	        // for the controller to warm up its caches even before it is elected as the leader. This
   163	        // improves leadership failover time, as the caches will be prepopulated before the controller
   164	        // transitions to be leader.
   165	        //
   166	        // Setting EnableWarmup to true and NeedLeaderElection to true means the controller will start its
   167	        // sources without waiting to become leader.
   168	        // Setting EnableWarmup to true and NeedLeaderElection to false is a no-op as controllers without
   169	        // leader election do not wait on leader election to start their sources.
   170	        // Defaults to false.
   171	        EnableWarmup *bool
   172	
   173	        ReconciliationTimeout time.Duration
   174	}
   175	
   176	// New returns a new Controller configured with the given options.
   177	func New[request comparable](options Options[request]) *Controller[request] {
   178	        return &Controller[request]{
   179	                Do:                      options.Do,
   180	                RateLimiter:             options.RateLimiter,
   181	                NewQueue:                options.NewQueue,
   182	                MaxConcurrentReconciles: options.MaxConcurrentReconciles,
   183	                CacheSyncTimeout:        options.CacheSyncTimeout,
   184	                Name:                    options.Name,
   185	                LogConstructor:          options.LogConstructor,
   186	                RecoverPanic:            options.RecoverPanic,
   187	                LeaderElected:           options.LeaderElected,
   188	                EnableWarmup:            options.EnableWarmup,
   189	                ReconciliationTimeout:   options.ReconciliationTimeout,
   190	        }
   191	}
   192	
   193	// Reconcile implements reconcile.Reconciler.
   194	func (c *Controller[request]) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req request) (_ reconcile.Result, err error) {
   195	        defer func() {
   196	                if r := recover(); r != nil {
   197	                        ctrlmetrics.ReconcilePanics.WithLabelValues(c.Name).Inc()
   198	
   199	                        if c.RecoverPanic == nil || *c.RecoverPanic {
   200	                                for _, fn := range utilruntime.PanicHandlers {
   201	                                        fn(ctx, r)
   202	                                }
   203	                                err = fmt.Errorf("panic: %v [recovered]", r)
   204	                                return
   205	                        }
   206	
   207	                        log := logf.FromContext(ctx)
   208	                        log.Info(fmt.Sprintf("Observed a panic in reconciler: %v", r))
   209	                        panic(r)
   210	                }
   211	        }()
   212	
   213	        var timeoutCause error
   214	        if c.ReconciliationTimeout > 0 {
   215	                timeoutCause = errReconciliationTimeout
   216	                var cancel context.CancelFunc
   217	                ctx, cancel = context.WithTimeoutCause(ctx, c.ReconciliationTimeout, timeoutCause)
   218	                defer cancel()
   219	        }
   220	
   221	        res, err := c.Do.Reconcile(ctx, req)
   222	
   223	        // Check if the reconciliation timed out due to our wrapper timeout guardrail.
   224	        // We check ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded first to ensure the context was actually
   225	        // cancelled due to a deadline (not parent cancellation or other reasons), then verify it was
   226	        // our specific timeout cause. This prevents false positives from parent context cancellations
   227	        // or other timeout scenarios.
   228	        if timeoutCause != nil && ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded && errors.Is(context.Cause(ctx), timeoutCause) {
   229	                ctrlmetrics.ReconcileTimeouts.WithLabelValues(c.Name).Inc()
   230	        }
   231	
   232	        return res, err
   233	}
   234	
   235	// Watch implements controller.Controller.
   236	func (c *Controller[request]) Watch(src source.TypedSource[request]) error {
   237	        c.mu.Lock()
   238	        defer c.mu.Unlock()
   239	
   240	        // Sources weren't started yet, store the watches locally and return.
   241	        // These sources are going to be held until either Warmup() or Start(...) is called.
   242	        if !c.startedEventSourcesAndQueue {
   243	                c.startWatches = append(c.startWatches, src)
   244	                return nil
   245	        }
   246	
   247	        c.LogConstructor(nil).Info("Starting EventSource", "source", src)
   248	        return src.Start(c.ctx, c.Queue)
   249	}
   250	
   251	// NeedLeaderElection implements the manager.LeaderElectionRunnable interface.
   252	func (c *Controller[request]) NeedLeaderElection() bool {
   253	        if c.LeaderElected == nil {
   254	                return true
   255	        }
   256	        return *c.LeaderElected
   257	}
   258	
   259	// Warmup implements the manager.WarmupRunnable interface.
   260	func (c *Controller[request]) Warmup(ctx context.Context) error {
   261	        if c.EnableWarmup == nil || !*c.EnableWarmup {
   262	                return nil
   263	        }
   264	
   265	        c.mu.Lock()
   266	        defer c.mu.Unlock()
   267	
   268	        // Set the ctx so later calls to watch use this internal context
   269	        c.ctx = ctx
   270	
   271	        return c.startEventSourcesAndQueueLocked(ctx)
   272	}
   273	
   274	// Start implements controller.Controller.
   275	func (c *Controller[request]) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   276	        // use an IIFE to get proper lock handling
   277	        // but lock outside to get proper handling of the queue shutdown
   278	        c.mu.Lock()
   279	        if c.Started {
   280	                return errors.New("controller was started more than once. This is likely to be caused by being added to a manager multiple times")
   281	        }
   282	
   283	        c.initMetrics()
   284	
   285	        // Set the internal context.
   286	        c.ctx = ctx
   287	
   288	        wg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
   289	        err := func() error {
   290	                defer c.mu.Unlock()
   291	
   292	                // TODO(pwittrock): Reconsider HandleCrash
   293	                defer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithLogger(c.LogConstructor(nil))
   294	
   295	                // NB(directxman12): launch the sources *before* trying to wait for the
   296	                // caches to sync so that they have a chance to register their intended
   297	                // caches.
   298	                if err := c.startEventSourcesAndQueueLocked(ctx); err != nil {
   299	                        return err
   300	                }
   301	
   302	                c.LogConstructor(nil).Info("Starting Controller")
   303	
   304	                // Launch workers to process resources
   305	                c.LogConstructor(nil).Info("Starting workers", "worker count", c.MaxConcurrentReconciles)
   306	                wg.Add(c.MaxConcurrentReconciles)
   307	                for i := 0; i < c.MaxConcurrentReconciles; i++ {
   308	                        go func() {
   309	                                defer wg.Done()
   310	                                // Run a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and marks them done.
   311	                                // It enforces that the reconcileHandler is never invoked concurrently with the same object.
   312	                                for c.processNextWorkItem(ctx) {
   313	                                }
   314	                        }()
   315	                }
   316	
   317	                c.Started = true
   318	                return nil
   319	        }()
   320	        if err != nil {
   321	                return err
   322	        }
   323	
   324	        <-ctx.Done()
   325	        c.LogConstructor(nil).Info("Shutdown signal received, waiting for all workers to finish")
   326	        wg.Wait()
   327	        c.LogConstructor(nil).Info("All workers finished")
   328	        return nil
   329	}
   330	
   331	// startEventSourcesAndQueueLocked launches all the sources registered with this controller and waits
   332	// for them to sync. It returns an error if any of the sources fail to start or sync.
   333	func (c *Controller[request]) startEventSourcesAndQueueLocked(ctx context.Context) error {
   334	        var retErr error
   335	
   336	        c.didStartEventSourcesOnce.Do(func() {
   337	                queue := c.NewQueue(c.Name, c.RateLimiter)
   338	                if priorityQueue, isPriorityQueue := queue.(priorityqueue.PriorityQueue[request]); isPriorityQueue {
   339	                        c.Queue = priorityQueue
   340	                } else {
   341	                        c.Queue = &priorityQueueWrapper[request]{TypedRateLimitingInterface: queue}
   342	                }
   343	                go func() {
   344	                        <-ctx.Done()
   345	                        c.Queue.ShutDown()
   346	                }()
   347	
   348	                errGroup := &errgroup.Group{}
   349	                for _, watch := range c.startWatches {
   350	                        log := c.LogConstructor(nil)
   351	                        _, ok := watch.(interface {
   352	                                String() string
   353	                        })
   354	                        if !ok {
   355	                                log = log.WithValues("source", fmt.Sprintf("%T", watch))
   356	                        } else {
   357	                                log = log.WithValues("source", fmt.Sprintf("%s", watch))
   358	                        }
   359	                        didStartSyncingSource := &atomic.Bool{}
   360	                        errGroup.Go(func() error {
   361	                                // Use a timeout for starting and syncing the source to avoid silently
   362	                                // blocking startup indefinitely if it doesn't come up.
   363	                                sourceStartCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.CacheSyncTimeout)
   364	                                defer cancel()
   365	
   366	                                sourceStartErrChan := make(chan error, 1) // Buffer chan to not leak goroutine if we time out
   367	                                go func() {
   368	                                        defer close(sourceStartErrChan)
   369	                                        log.Info("Starting EventSource")
   370	
   371	                                        if err := watch.Start(ctx, c.Queue); err != nil {
   372	                                                sourceStartErrChan <- err
   373	                                                return
   374	                                        }
   375	                                        syncingSource, ok := watch.(source.TypedSyncingSource[request])
   376	                                        if !ok {
   377	                                                return
   378	                                        }
   379	                                        didStartSyncingSource.Store(true)
   380	                                        if err := syncingSource.WaitForSync(sourceStartCtx); err != nil {
   381	                                                err := fmt.Errorf("failed to wait for %s caches to sync %v: %w", c.Name, syncingSource, err)
   382	                                                log.Error(err, "Could not wait for Cache to sync")
   383	                                                sourceStartErrChan <- err
   384	                                        }
   385	                                }()
   386	
   387	                                select {
   388	                                case err := <-sourceStartErrChan:
   389	                                        return err
   390	                                case <-sourceStartCtx.Done():
   391	                                        if didStartSyncingSource.Load() { // We are racing with WaitForSync, wait for it to let it tell us what happened
   392	                                                return <-sourceStartErrChan
   393	                                        }
   394	                                        if ctx.Err() != nil { // Don't return an error if the root context got cancelled
   395	                                                return nil
   396	                                        }
   397	                                        return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for source %s to Start. Please ensure that its Start() method is non-blocking", watch)
   398	                                }
   399	                        })
   400	                }
   401	                retErr = errGroup.Wait()
   402	
   403	                // All the watches have been started, we can reset the local slice.
   404	                //
   405	                // We should never hold watches more than necessary, each watch source can hold a backing cache,
   406	                // which won't be garbage collected if we hold a reference to it.
   407	                c.startWatches = nil
   408	
   409	                // Mark event sources as started after resetting the startWatches slice so that watches from
   410	                // a new Watch() call are immediately started.
   411	                c.startedEventSourcesAndQueue = true
   412	        })
   413	
   414	        return retErr
   415	}
   416	
   417	// processNextWorkItem will read a single work item off the workqueue and
   418	// attempt to process it, by calling the reconcileHandler.
   419	func (c *Controller[request]) processNextWorkItem(ctx context.Context) bool {
   420	        obj, priority, shutdown := c.Queue.GetWithPriority()
   421	        if shutdown {
   422	                // Stop working
   423	                return fals<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    38	var logInformer = logf.RuntimeLog.WithName("source").WithName("Informer")
    39	
    40	// Source is a source of events (e.g. Create, Update, Delete operations on Kubernetes Objects, Webhook callbacks, etc)
    41	// which should be processed by event.EventHandlers to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
    42	//
    43	// * Use Kind for events originating in the cluster (e.g. Pod Create, Pod Update, Deployment Update).
    44	//
    45	// * Use Channel for events originating outside the cluster (e.g. GitHub Webhook callback, Polling external urls).
    46	//
    47	// Users may build their own Source implementations.
    48	type Source = TypedSource[reconcile.Request]
    49	
    50	// TypedSource is a generic source of events (e.g. Create, Update, Delete operations on Kubernetes Objects, Webhook callbacks, etc)
    51	// which should be processed by event.EventHandlers to enqueue a request.
    52	//
    53	// * Use Kind for events originating in the cluster (e.g. Pod Create, Pod Update, Deployment Update).
    54	//
    55	// * Use Channel for events originating outside the cluster (e.g. GitHub Webhook callback, Polling external urls).
    56	//
    57	// Users may build their own Source implementations.
    58	type TypedSource[request comparable] interface {
    59	        // Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to start the source.
    60	        // Start must be non-blocking.
    61	        Start(context.Context, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error
    62	}
    63	
    64	// SyncingSource is a source that needs syncing prior to being usable. The controller
    65	// will call its WaitForSync prior to starting workers.
    66	type SyncingSource = TypedSyncingSource[reconcile.Request]
    67	
    68	// TypedSyncingSource is a source that needs syncing prior to being usable. The controller
    69	// will call its WaitForSync prior to starting workers.
    70	type TypedSyncingSource[request comparable] interface {
    71	        TypedSource[request]
    72	        WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error
    73	}
    74	
    75	// Kind creates a KindSource with the given cache provider.
    76	func Kind[object client.Object](
    77	        cache cache.Cache,
    78	        obj object,
    79	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, reconcile.Request],
    80	        predicates ...predicate.TypedPredicate[object],
    81	) SyncingSource {
    82	        return TypedKind(cache, obj, handler, predicates...)
    83	}
    84	
    85	// TypedKind creates a KindSource with the given cache provider.
    86	func TypedKind[object client.Object, request comparable](
    87	        cache cache.Cache,
    88	        obj object,
    89	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request],
    90	        predicates ...predicate.TypedPredicate[object],
    91	) TypedSyncingSource[request] {
    92	        return &internal.Kind[object, request]{
    93	                Type:       obj,
    94	                Cache:      cache,
    95	                Handler:    handler,
    96	                Predicates: predicates,
    97	        }
    98	}
    99	
   100	var _ Source = &channel[string, reconcile.Request]{}
   101	
   102	// ChannelOpt allows to configure a source.Channel.
   103	type ChannelOpt[object any, request comparable] func(*channel[object, request])
   104	
   105	// WithPredicates adds the configured predicates to a source.Channel.
   106	func WithPredicates[object any, request comparable](p ...predicate.TypedPredicate[object]) ChannelOpt[object, request] {
   107	        return func(c *channel[object, request]) {
   108	                c.predicates = append(c.predicates, p...)
   109	        }
   110	}
   111	
   112	// WithBufferSize configures the buffer size for a source.Channel. By
   113	// default, the buffer size is 1024.
   114	func WithBufferSize[object any, request comparable](bufferSize int) ChannelOpt[object, request] {
   115	        return func(c *channel[object, request]) {
   116	                c.bufferSize = &bufferSize
   117	        }
   118	}
   119	
   120	// Channel is used to provide a source of events originating outside the cluster
   121	// (e.g. GitHub Webhook callback).  Channel requires the user to wire the external
   122	// source (e.g. http handler) to write GenericEvents to the underlying channel.
   123	func Channel[object any](
   124	        source <-chan event.TypedGenericEvent[object],
   125	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, reconcile.Request],
   126	        opts ...ChannelOpt[object, reconcile.Request],
   127	) Source {
   128	        return TypedChannel[object, reconcile.Request](source, handler, opts...)
   129	}
   130	
   131	// TypedChannel is used to provide a source of events originating outside the cluster
   132	// (e.g. GitHub Webhook callback).  Channel requires the user to wire the external
   133	// source (e.g. http handler) to write GenericEvents to the underlying channel.
   134	func TypedChannel[object any, request comparable](
   135	        source <-chan event.TypedGenericEvent[object],
   136	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request],
   137	        opts ...ChannelOpt[object, request],
   138	) TypedSource[request] {
   139	        c := &channel[object, request]{
   140	                source:  source,
   141	                handler: handler,
   142	        }
   143	        for _, opt := range opts {
   144	                opt(c)
   145	        }
   146	
   147	        return c
   148	}
   149	
   150	type channel[object any, request comparable] struct {
   151	        // once ensures the event distribution goroutine will be performed only once
   152	        once sync.Once
   153	
   154	        // source is the source channel to fetch GenericEvents
   155	        source <-chan event.TypedGenericEvent[object]
   156	
   157	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request]
   158	
   159	        predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
   160	
   161	        bufferSize *int
   162	
   163	        // dest is the destination channels of the added event handlers
   164	        dest []chan event.TypedGenericEvent[object]
   165	
   166	        // destLock is to ensure the destination channels are safely added/removed
   167	        destLock sync.Mutex
   168	}
   169	
   170	func (cs *channel[object, request]) String() string {
   171	        return fmt.Sprintf("channel source: %p", cs)
   172	}
   173	
   174	// Start implements Source and should only be called by the Controller.
   175	func (cs *channel[object, request]) Start(
   176	        ctx context.Context,
   177	        queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request],
   178	) error {
   179	        // Source should have been specified by the user.
   180	        if cs.source == nil {
   181	                return fmt.Errorf("must specify Channel.Source")
   182	        }
   183	        if cs.handler == nil {
   184	                return errors.New("must specify Channel.Handler")
   185	        }
   186	
   187	        if cs.bufferSize == nil {
   188	                cs.bufferSize = new(1024)
   189	        }
   190	
   191	        dst := make(chan event.TypedGenericEvent[object], *cs.bufferSize)
   192	
   193	        cs.destLock.Lock()
   194	        cs.dest = append(cs.dest, dst)
   195	        cs.destLock.Unlock()
   196	
   197	        cs.once.Do(func() {
   198	                // Distribute GenericEvents to all EventHandler / Queue pairs Watching this source
   199	                go cs.syncLoop(ctx)
   200	        })
   201	
   202	        go func() {
   203	                for evt := range dst {
   204	                        shouldHandle := true
   205	                        for _, p := range cs.predicates {
   206	                                if !p.Generic(evt) {
   207	                                        shouldHandle = false
   208	                                        break
   209	                                }
   210	                        }
   211	
   212	                        if shouldHandle {
   213	                                func() {
   214	                                        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
   215	                                        defer cancel()
   216	                                        cs.handler.Generic(ctx, evt, queue)
   217	                                }()
   218	                        }
   219	                }
   220	        }()
   221	
   222	        return nil
   223	}
   224	
   225	func (cs *channel[object, request]) doStop() {
   226	        cs.destLock.Lock()
   227	        defer cs.destLock.Unlock()
   228	
   229	        for _, dst := range cs.dest {
   230	                close(dst)
   231	        }
   232	}
   233	
   234	func (cs *channel[object, request]) distribute(evt event.TypedGenericEvent[object]) {
   235	        cs.destLock.Lock()
   236	        defer cs.destLock.Unlock()
   237	
   238	        for _, dst := range cs.dest {
   239	                // We cannot make it under goroutine here, or we'll meet the
   240	                // race condition of writing message to closed channels.
   241	                // To avoid blocking, the dest channels are expected to be of
   242	                // proper buffer size. If we still see it blocked, then
   243	                // the controller is thought to be in an abnormal state.
   244	                dst <- evt
   245	        }
   246	}
   247	
   248	func (cs *channel[object, request]) syncLoop(ctx context.Context) {
   249	        for {
   250	                select {
   251	                case <-ctx.Done():
   252	                        // Close destination channels
   253	                        cs.doStop()
   254	                        return
   255	                case evt, stillOpen := <-cs.source:
   256	                        if !stillOpen {
   257	                                // if the source channel is closed, we're never gonna get
   258	                                // anything more on it, so stop & bail
   259	                                cs.doStop()
   260	                                return
   261	                        }
   262	                        cs.distribute(evt)
   263	                }
   264	        }
   265	}
   266	
   267	// Informer is used to provide a source of events originating inside the cluster from Watches (e.g. Pod Create).
   268	type Informer struct {
   269	        // Informer is the controller-runtime Informer
   270	        Informer   cache.Informer
   271	        Handler    handler.EventHandler
   272	        Predicates []predicate.Predicate
   273	}
   274	
   275	var _ Source = &Informer{}
   276	
   277	// Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to register an EventHandler with the Informer
   278	// to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
   279	func (is *Informer) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) error {
   280	        // Informer should have been specified by the user.
   281	        if is.Informer == nil {
   282	                return fmt.Errorf("must specify Informer.Informer")
   283	        }
   284	        if is.Handler == nil {
   285	                return errors.New("must specify Informer.Handler")
   286	        }
   287	
   288	        _, err := is.Informer.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(internal.NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, is.Handler, is.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
   289	                Logger: &logInformer,
   290	        })
   291	        if err != nil {
   292	                return err
   293	        }
   294	        return nil
   295	}
   296	
   297	func (is *Informer) String() string {
   298	        return fmt.Sprintf("informer source: %p", is.Informer)
   299	}
   300	
   301	var _ Source = Func(nil)
   302	
   303	// Func is a function that implements Source.
   304	type Func = TypedFunc[reconcile.Request]
   305	
   306	// TypedFunc is a function that implements Source.
   307	type TypedFunc[request comparable] func(context.Context, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error
   308	
   309	// Start implements Source.
   310	func (f TypedFunc[request]) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error {
   311	        return f(ctx, queue)
   312	}
   313	
   314	func (f TypedFunc[request]) String() string {
   315	        return fmt.Sprintf("func source: %p", f)
   316	}
   317

   175	}
   176	
   177	// WatchesMetadata is the same as Watches, but forces the internal cache to only watch PartialObjectMetadata.
   178	//
   179	// This is useful when watching lots of objects, really big objects, or objects for which you only know
   180	// the GVK, but not the structure. You'll need to pass metav1.PartialObjectMetadata to the client
   181	// when fetching objects in your reconciler, otherwise you'll end up with a duplicate structured or unstructured cache.
   182	//
   183	// When watching a resource with metadata only, for example the v1.Pod, you should not Get and List using the v1.Pod type.
   184	// Instead, you should use the special metav1.PartialObjectMetadata type.
   185	//
   186	// ❌ Incorrect:
   187	//
   188	//      pod := &v1.Pod{}
   189	//      mgr.GetClient().Get(ctx, nsAndName, pod)
   190	//
   191	// ✅ Correct:
   192	//
   193	//      pod := &metav1.PartialObjectMetadata{}
   194	//      pod.SetGroupVersionKind(schema.GroupVersionKind{
   195	//          Group:   "",
   196	//          Version: "v1",
   197	//          Kind:    "Pod",
   198	//      })
   199	//      mgr.GetClient().Get(ctx, nsAndName, pod)
   200	//
   201	// In the first case, controller-runtime will create another cache for the
   202	// concrete type on top of the metadata cache; this increases memory
   203	// consumption and leads to race conditions as caches are not in sync.
   204	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) WatchesMetadata(
   205	        object client.Object,
   206	        eventHandler handler.TypedEventHandler[client.Object, request],
   207	        opts ...WatchesOption,
   208	) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   209	        opts = append(opts, OnlyMetadata)
   210	        return blder.Watches(object, eventHandler, opts...)
   211	}
   212	
   213	// WatchesRawSource exposes the lower-level ControllerManagedBy Watches functions through the builder.
   214	//
   215	// WatchesRawSource does not respect predicates configured through WithEventFilter.
   216	//
   217	// WatchesRawSource makes it possible to use typed handlers and predicates with `source.Kind` as well as custom source implementations.
   218	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) WatchesRawSource(src source.TypedSource[request]) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   219	        blder.rawSources = append(blder.rawSources, src)
   220	
   221	        return blder
   222	}
   223	
   224	// WithEventFilter sets the event filters, to filter which create/update/delete/generic events eventually
   225	// trigger reconciliations. For example, filtering on whether the resource version has changed.
   226	// Given predicate is added for all watched objects and thus must be able to deal with the type
   227	// of all watched objects.
   228	//
   229	// Defaults to the empty list.
   230	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) WithEventFilter(p predicate.Predicate) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   231	        blder.globalPredicates = append(blder.globalPredicates, p)
   232	        return blder
   233	}

   235	// WithOptions overrides the controller options used in doController. Defaults to empty.
   236	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) WithOptions(options controller.TypedOptions[request]) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   237	        blder.ctrlOptions = options
   238	        return blder
   239	}

   242	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) WithLogConstructor(logConstructor func(*request) logr.Logger) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   243	        blder.ctrlOptions.LogConstructor = logConstructor
   244	        return blder
   245	}
