   247	// Named sets the name of the controller to the given name. The name shows up
   248	// in metrics, among other things, and thus should be a prometheus compatible name
   249	// (underscores and alphanumeric characters only).
   250	//
   251	// By default, controllers are named using the lowercase version of their kind.
   252	//
   253	// The name must be unique as it is used to identify the controller in metrics and logs.
   254	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) Named(name string) *TypedBuilder[request] {
   255	        blder.name = name
   256	        return blder
   257	}
   258	
   259	// Complete builds the Application Controller.
   260	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) Complete(r reconcile.TypedReconciler[request]) error {
   261	        _, err := blder.Build(r)
   262	        return err
   263	}
   264	
   265	// Build builds the Application Controller and returns the Controller it created.
   266	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) Build(r reconcile.TypedReconciler[request]) (controller.TypedController[request], error) {
   267	        if r == nil {
   268	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("must provide a non-nil Reconciler")
   269	        }
   270	        if blder.mgr == nil {
   271	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("must provide a non-nil Manager")
   272	        }
   273	        if blder.forInput.err != nil {
   274	                return nil, blder.forInput.err
   275	        }
   276	
   277	        // Set the ControllerManagedBy
   278	        if err := blder.doController(r); err != nil {
   279	                return nil, err
   280	        }
   281	
   282	        // Set the Watch
   283	        if err := blder.doWatch(); err != nil {
   284	                return nil, err
   285	        }
   286	
   287	        return blder.ctrl, nil
   288	}
   289	
   290	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) project(obj client.Object, proj objectProjection) (client.Object, error) {
   291	        switch proj {
   292	        case projectAsNormal:
   293	                return obj, nil
   294	        case projectAsMetadata:
   295	                metaObj := &metav1.PartialObjectMetadata{}
   296	                gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(obj, blder.mgr.GetScheme())
   297	                if err != nil {
   298	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to determine GVK of %T for a metadata-only watch: %w", obj, err)
   299	                }
   300	                metaObj.SetGroupVersionKind(gvk)
   301	                return metaObj, nil
   302	        default:
   303	                panic(fmt.Sprintf("unexpected projection type %v on type %T, should not be possible since this is an internal field", proj, obj))
   304	        }
   305	}
   306	
   307	func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) doWatch() error {
   308	        // Reconcile type
   309	        if blder.forInput.object != nil {
   310	                obj, err := blder.project(blder.forInput.object, blder.forInput.objectProjection)




2026-05-30 01:08:20,167 - INFO - swea-agent-kubernetes-sigs__controller-runtime-3494 - ========================= STEP 14 =========================
2026-05-30 01:08:20,171 - DEBUG - swea-lm-kubernetes-sigs__controller-runtime-3494 - n_cache_control: 2
2026-05-30 01:08:25,466 - DEBUG - swea-lm-kubernetes-sigs__controller-runtime-3494 - Response: ModelResponse(id='8e0c8ac020214c819811221d9c28d8c4', created=1780103301, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content='Now I understand the full picture. Now I understand the full picture. The `PartialObjectMetadata` has its GVK set (e.g., `v1.Pod`), but when it's logged with `%T`, it shows `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata` instead of the actual GVK.

The fix should be to use the GVK from the object when it's a `PartialObjectMetadata` (or more generally, extract the GVK from the object). Let me look at how we can get the GVK from a `PartialObjectMetadata`:

The `PartialObjectMetadata` has a `TypeMeta` that contains the GVK. We can use `apiutil.GVKForObject` to extract it.

    37	// NewEventHandler creates a new EventHandler.
    38	func NewEventHandler[object client.Object, request comparable](
    39	        ctx context.Context,
    40	        queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request],
    41	        handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request],
    42	        predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]) *EventHandler[object, request] {
    43	        return &EventHandler[object, request]{
    44	                ctx:        ctx,
    45	                handler:    handler,
    46	                queue:      queue,
    47	                predicates: predicates,
    48	        }
    49	}
    50	
    51	// EventHandler adapts a handler.EventHandler interface to a cache.ResourceEventHandler interface.
    52	type EventHandler[object client.Object, request comparable] struct {
    53	        // ctx stores the context that created the event handler
    54	        // that is used to propagate cancellation signals to each handler function.
    55	        ctx context.Context
    56	
    57	        handler    handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request]
    58	        queue      workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]
    59	        predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
    60	}
    61	
    62	// OnAdd creates CreateEvent and calls Create on EventHandler.
    63	func (e *EventHandler[object, request]) OnAdd(obj any, isInInitialList bool) {
    64	        c := event.TypedCreateEvent[object]{
    65	                IsInInitialList: isInInitialList,
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        // Pull Object out of the object
    69	        if o, ok := obj.(object); ok {
    70	                c.Object = o
    71	        } else {
    72	                log.Error(nil, "OnAdd missing Object",
    73	                        "object", obj, "type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj))
    74	                return
    75	        }
    76	
    77	        for _, p := range e.predicates {
    78	                if !p.Create(c) {
    79	                        return
    80	                }
    81	        }
    82	
    83	        // Invoke create handler
    84	        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(e.ctx)
    85	        defer cancel()
    86	        e.handler.Create(ctx, c, e.queue)
    87	}
    88	
    89	// OnUpdate creates UpdateEvent and calls Update on EventHandler.
    90	func (e *EventHandler[object, request]) OnUpdate(oldObj, newObj any) {
    91	        u := event.TypedUpdateEvent[object]{}
    92	
    93	        if o, ok := oldObj.(object); ok {
    94	                u.ObjectOld = o
    95	        } else {
    96	                log.Error(nil, "OnUpdate missing ObjectOld",
    97	                        "object", oldObj, "type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", oldObj))
    98	                return
    99	        }
   100	
   101	        // Pull Object out of the object
   102	        if o, ok := newObj.(object); ok {
   103	                u.ObjectNew = o
   104	        } else {
   105	                log.Error(nil, "OnUpdate missing ObjectNew",
   106	                        "object", newObj, "type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", newObj))
   107	                return
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        for _, p := range e.predicates {
   111	                if !p.Update(u) {
   112	                        return
   113	                }
   114	        }
   115	
   116	        // Invoke update handler
   117	        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(e.ctx)
   118	        defer cancel()
   119	        e.handler.Update(ctx, u, e.queue)
   120	}
   121	
   122	// OnDelete creates DeleteEvent and calls Delete on EventHandler.
   123	func (e *EventHandler[object, request]) OnDelete(obj any) {
   124	        d := event.TypedDeleteEvent[object]{}
   125	
   126	        // Deal with tombstone events by pulling the object out.  Tombstone events wrap the object in a
   127	        // DeleteFinalStateUnknown struct, so the object needs to be pulled out.
   128	        // Copied from sample-controller
   129	        // This should never happen if we aren't missing events, which we have concluded that we are not
   130	        // and made decisions off of this belief.  Maybe this shouldn't be here?
   131	        var ok bool
   132	        if _, ok = obj.(client.Object); !ok {
   133	                // If the object doesn't have Metadata, assume it is a tombstone object of type DeletedFinalStateUnknown
   134	                tombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)
   135	                if !ok {
   136	                        log.Error(nil, "Error decoding objects.  Expected cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown",
   137	                                "type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj),
   138	                                "object", obj)
   139	                        return
   140	                }
   141	
   142	                // Set DeleteStateUnknown to true
   143	                d.DeleteStateUnknown = true
   144	
   145	                // Set obj to the tombstone obj
   146	                obj = tombstone.Obj
   147	        }
   148	
   149	        // Pull Object out of the object
   150	        if o, ok := obj.(object); ok {
   151	                d.Object = o
   152	        } else {
   153	                log.Error(nil, "OnDelete missing Object",
   154	                        "object", obj, "type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj))
   155	                return
   156	        }
   157	
   158	        for _, p := range e.predicates {
   159	                if !p.Delete(d) {
   160	                        return
   161	                }
   162	        }
   163	
   164	        // Invoke delete handler
   165	        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(e.ctx)
   166	        defer cancel()
   167	        e.handler.Delete(ctx, d, e.queue)
   168	}

    17	// Package apiutil contains utilities for working with raw Kubernetes
    18	// API machinery, such as creating RESTMappers and raw REST clients,
    19	// and extracting the GVK of an object.
    20	package apiutil
    21	
    22	import (
    23	        "errors"
    24	        "fmt"
    25	        "net/http"
    26	        "reflect"
    27	        "sync"
    28	
    29	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
    30	        metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
    31	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    32	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
    33	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer"
    34	        "k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
    35	        clientgoscheme "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme"
    36	        "k8s.io/client-go/rest"
    37	)
    38	
    39	var (
    40	        protobufScheme     = runtime.NewScheme()
    41	        protobufSchemeLock sync.RWMutex
    42	)
    43	
    44	func init() {
    45	        // Currently only enabled for built-in resources which are guaranteed to implement Protocol Buffers.
    46	        // For custom resources, CRDs can not support Protocol Buffers but Aggregated API can.
    47	        // See doc: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/extend-kubernetes/api-extension/custom-resources/#advanced-features-and-flexibility
    48	        if err := clientgoscheme.AddToScheme(protobufScheme); err != nil {
    49	                panic(err)
    50	        }
    51	}
    52	
    53	// AddToProtobufScheme add the given SchemeBuilder into protobufScheme, which should
    54	// be additional types that do support protobuf.
    55	func AddToProtobufScheme(addToScheme func(*runtime.Scheme) error) error {
    56	        protobufSchemeLock.Lock()
    57	        defer protobufSchemeLock.Unlock()
    58	        return addToScheme(protobufScheme)
    59	}
    60	
    61	// IsObjectNamespaced returns true if the object is namespace scoped.
    62	// For unstructured objects the gvk is found from the object itself.
    63	func IsObjectNamespaced(obj runtime.Object, scheme *runtime.Scheme, restmapper meta.RESTMapper) (bool, error) {
    64	        gvk, err := GVKForObject(obj, scheme)
    65	        if err != nil {
    66	                return false, err
    67	        }
    68	
    69	        return IsGVKNamespaced(gvk, restmapper)
    70	}
    71	
    72	// IsGVKNamespaced returns true if the object having the provided
    73	// GVK is namespace scoped.
    74	func IsGVKNamespaced(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, restmapper meta.RESTMapper) (bool, error) {
    75	        // Fetch the RESTMapping using the complete GVK. If we exclude the Version, the Version set
    76	        // will be populated using the cached Group if available. This can lead to failures updating
    77	        // the cache with new Versions of CRDs registered at runtime.
    78	        restmapping, err := restmapper.RESTMapping(schema.GroupKind{Group: gvk.Group, Kind: gvk.Kind}, gvk.Version)
    79	        if err != nil {
    80	                return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to get restmapping: %w", err)
    81	        }
    82	
    83	        scope := restmapping.Scope.Name()
    84	        if scope == "" {
    85	                return false, errors.New("scope cannot be identified, empty scope returned")
    86	        }
    87	
    88	        if scope != meta.RESTScopeNameRoot {
    89	                return true, nil
    90	        }
    91	        return false, nil
    92	}
    93	
    94	// GVKForObject finds the GroupVersionKind associated with the given object, if there is only a single such GVK.
    95	func GVKForObject(obj runtime.Object, scheme *runtime.Scheme) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {
    96	        // TODO(directxman12): do we want to generalize this to arbitrary container types?
    97	        // I think we'd need a generalized form of scheme or something.  It's a
    98	        // shame there's not a reliable "GetGVK" interface that works by default
    99	        // for unpopulated static types and populated "dynamic" types
   100	        // (unstructured, partial, etc)
   101	
   102	        // check for PartialObjectMetadata, which is analogous to unstructured, but isn't handled by ObjectKinds
   103	        _, isPartial := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata)
   104	        _, isPartialList := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList)
   105	        if isPartial || isPartialList {
   106	                // we require that the GVK be populated in order to recognize the object
   107	                gvk := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
   108	                if len(gvk.Kind) == 0 {
   109	                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingKindErr("unstructured object has no kind")
   110	                }
   111	                if len(gvk.Version) == 0 {
   112	                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingVersionErr("unstructured object has no version")
   113	                }
   114	                return gvk, nil
   115	        }
   116	
   117	        // Use the given scheme to retrieve all the GVKs for the object.
   118	        gvks, isUnversioned, err := scheme.ObjectKinds(obj)
   119	        if err != nil {
   120	                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err
   121	        }
   122	        if isUnversioned {
   123	                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("cannot create group-version-kind for unversioned type %T", obj)
   124	        }
   125	
   126	        switch {
   127	        case len(gvks) < 1:
   128	                // If the object has no GVK, the object might not have been registered with the scheme.
   129	                // or it's not a valid object.
   130	                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("no GroupVersionKind associated with Go type %T, was the type registered with the Scheme?", obj)
   131	        case len(gvks) > 1:
   132	                err := fmt.Errorf("multiple GroupVersionKinds associated with Go type %T within the Scheme, this can happen when a type is registered for multiple GVKs at the same time", obj)
   133	
   134	                // We've found multiple GVKs for the object.
   135	                currentGVK := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
   136	                if !currentGVK.Empty() {
   137	                        // If the base object has a GVK, check if it's in the list of GVKs before using it.
   138	                        for _, gvk := range gvks {
   139	                                if gvk == currentGVK {
   140	                                        return gvk, nil
   141	                                }
   142	                        }
   143	
   144	                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
   145	                                "%w: the object's supplied GroupVersionKind %q was not found in the Scheme's list; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, currentGVK, gvks)
   146	                }
   147	
   148	                // This should only trigger for things like metav1.XYZ --
   149	                // normal versioned types should be fine.
   150	                //
   151	                // See https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/362
   152	                // for more information.
   153	                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
   154	                        "%w: callers can either fix their type registration to only register it once, or specify the GroupVersionKind to use for object passed in; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, gvks)
   155	        default:
   156	                // In any other case, we've found a single GVK for the object.
   157	                return gvks[0], nil
   158	        }
   159	}
   160	
   161	// RESTClientForGVK constructs a new rest.Interface capable of accessing the resource associated
   162	// with the given GroupVersionKind. The REST client will be configured to use the negotiated serializer from
   163	// baseConfig, if set, otherwise a default serializer will be set.
   164	func RESTClientForGVK(
   165	        gvk schema.GroupVersionKind,
   166	        forceDisableProtoBuf bool,
   167	        isUnstructured bool,
   168	        baseConfig *rest.Config,
   169	        codecs serializer.CodecFactory,
   170	        httpClient *http.Client,
   171	) (rest.Interface, error) {
   172	        if httpClient == nil {
   173	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("httpClient must not be nil, consider using rest.HTTPClientFor(c) to create a client")
   174	        }
   175	        return rest.RESTClientForConfigAndClient(createRestConfig(gvk, forceDisableProtoBuf, isUnstructured, baseConfig, codecs), httpClient)
   176	}
   177	
   178	// createRestConfig copies the base config and updates needed fields for a new rest config.
   179	func createRestConfig(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind,
   180	        forceDisableProtoBuf bool,
   181	        isUnstructured bool,
   182	        baseConfig *rest.Config,
   183	        codecs serializer.CodecFactory,
   184	) *rest.Config {
   185	        gv := gvk.GroupVersion()
   186	
   187	        cfg := rest.CopyConfig(baseConfig)
   188	        cfg.GroupVersion = &gv
   189	        if gvk.Group == "" {
   190	                cfg.APIPath = "/api"
   191	        } else {
   192	                cfg.APIPath = "/apis"
   193	        }
   194	        if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
   195	                cfg.UserAgent = rest.DefaultKubernetesUserAgent()
   196	        }
   197	        // TODO(FillZpp): In the long run, we want to check discovery or something to make sure that this is actually true.
   198	        if cfg.ContentType == "" && !forceDisableProtoBuf {
   199	                protobufSchemeLock.RLock()
   200	                if protobufScheme.Recognizes(gvk) {
   201	                        cfg.ContentType = runtime.ContentTypeProtobuf
   202	                }
   203	                protobufSchemeLock.RUnlock()
   204	        }
   205	
   206	        if isUnstructured {
   207	                // If the object is unstructured, we use the client-go dynamic serializer.
   208	                cfg = dynamic.ConfigFor(cfg)
   209	        } else {
   210	                cfg.NegotiatedSerializer = serializerWithTargetZeroingDecode{NegotiatedSerializer: serializer.WithoutConversionCodecFactory{CodecFactory: codecs}}
   211	        }
   212	
   213	        return cfg
   214	}
   215	
   216	type serializerWithTargetZeroingDecode struct {
   217	        runtime.NegotiatedSerializer
   218	}
   219	
   220	func (s serializerWithTargetZeroingDecode) DecoderToVersion(serializer runtime.Decoder, r runtime.GroupVersioner) runtime.Decoder {
   221	        return targetZeroingDecoder{upstream: s.NegotiatedSerializer.DecoderToVersion(serializer, r)}
   222	}
   223	
   224	type targetZeroingDecoder struct {
   225	        upstream runtime.Decoder
   226	}
   227	
   228	func (t targetZeroingDecoder) Decode(data []byte, defaults *schema.GroupVersionKind, into runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, *schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {
   229	        zero(into)
   230	        return t.upstream.Decode(data, defaults, into)
   231	}
   232	
   233	// zero zeros the value of a pointer.
   234	func zero(x any) {
   235	        if x == nil {
   236	                return
   237	        }
   238	        res := reflect.ValueOf(x).Elem()
   239	        res.Set(reflect.Zero(res.Type()))
   240	}
   241

Now let me create a script to reproduce the issue. But first, let me understand the approach. The problem is:

1. When `PartialObjectMetadata` is used with a GVK set (e.g., `Pod`), the logs show `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata` instead of the actual kind like `Pod`.

2. The key places where this needs to be fixed:
   - `pkg/internal/source/kind.go` - `String()` method (line 120) and `WaitForSync` error (line 136)
   - `pkg/internal/controller/controller.go` - line 355 for `%T` formatting fallback
   - `pkg/cache/internal/informers.go` - line 332 for the timeout error

The solution should create a helper function that, given an object, returns a human-readable string that includes the actual GVK when it's a `PartialObjectMetadata`. Let me check if there's already such a helper or if we need to add one.

    40	var _ = Describe("Internal", func() {

    44	        BeforeEach(func(ctx SpecContext) {
    45	                funcs = &handler.Funcs{
    46	                        CreateFunc: func(context.Context, event.CreateEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    47	                                defer GinkgoRecover()
    48	                                Fail("Did not expect CreateEvent to be called.")
    49	                        },
    50	                        DeleteFunc: func(context.Context, event.DeleteEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    51	                                defer GinkgoRecover()
    52	                                Fail("Did not expect DeleteEvent to be called.")
    53	                        },
    54	                        UpdateFunc: func(context.Context, event.UpdateEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    55	                                defer GinkgoRecover()
    56	                                Fail("Did not expect UpdateEvent to be called.")
    57	                        },
    58	                        GenericFunc: func(context.Context, event.GenericEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    59	                                defer GinkgoRecover()
    60	                                Fail("Did not expect GenericEvent to be called.")
    61	                        },
    62	                }
    63	
    64	                setfuncs = &handler.Funcs{
    65	                        CreateFunc: func(context.Context, event.CreateEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    66	                                set = true
    67	                        },
    68	                        DeleteFunc: func(context.Context, event.DeleteEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    69	                                set = true
    70	                        },
    71	                        UpdateFunc: func(context.Context, event.UpdateEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    72	                                set = true
    73	                        },
    74	                        GenericFunc: func(context.Context, event.GenericEvent, workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[reconcile.Request]) {
    75	                                set = true
    76	                        },
    77	                }
    78	                instance = internal.NewEventHandler(ctx, &controllertest.Queue{}, funcs, nil)
    79	        })
    80	
    81	        Describe("EventHandler", func() {

   106	                        set = false

Let me think about the best approach:

1. We could create a helper function like `safeTypeName(obj runtime.Object) string` that returns a string with the GVK for `PartialObjectMetadata` and `%T` for other types.

2. Or we could modify the `String()` method of `Kind` to use GVK-based formatting.

Looking at the PR description, the issue is specifically about `PartialObjectMetadata`. The user sees `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata` instead of something like `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata (v1.Pod)` or just `v1.Pod`.

Let me look at how the builder projects to metadata:

From `builder/controller.go` line 290-305:
```go
func (blder *TypedBuilder[request]) project(obj client.Object, proj objectProjection) (client.Object, error) {
    switch proj {
    case projectAsNormal:
        return obj, nil
    case projectAsMetadata:
        metaObj := &metav1.PartialObjectMetadata{}
        gvk, err := apiutil.GVKForObject(obj, blder.mgr.GetScheme())
        if err != nil {
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to determine GVK of %T for a metadata-only watch: %w", obj, err)
        }
        metaObj.SetGroupVersionKind(gvk)
        return metaObj, nil
    ...
```

So the `PartialObjectMetadata` object has its GVK set. We can extract it.

Let me now think about the approach more concretely:

The root issue is that when using `PartialObjectMetadata`, the Go type `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata` is logged instead of the actual resource kind (e.g., `Pod`). 

The most maintainable approach would be to add a helper function that:
1. For `*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata` and `*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList`, extracts the GVK and returns it in a readable format
2. Falls back to `%T` for other types

But actually, looking at the PR description more carefully, the problem is broader. Even for regular types, the log shows `*v1.PartialObjectMetadata` when metadata-only watch is used. The solution should show the actual underlying kind.

Let me look at what `String()` methods currently exist for sources and how they're used in logging:

1. `kind.go:118-123` - `Kind.String()` returns `"kind source: %T"` 
2. `source.go:170-172` - `channel.String()` returns `"channel source: %p"`
3. `source.go:297-299` - `Informer.String()` returns `"informer source: %p"`
4. `source.go:314-316` - `Func.String()` returns `"func source: %p"`

The controller.go line 355 uses `%T` as a fallback when the source doesn't implement `String()`.

So the key fix should be in `Kind.String()` method and `WaitForSync` error message. Also the informers.go timeout error message.

Let me create a utility function that returns a human-readable type string for a runtime.Object:

I think the best approach is:
1. Create a function like `safeTypeName(obj runtime.Object) string` in the `internal` package (or in `apiutil`)
2. Use it in all the places where `%T` is currently used for objects that could be `PartialObjectMetadata`

But wait, let me check if there's already a function that does something similar. Let me search for `GetObjectKind`:

// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
// workers until the cache is synced.
func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
        select {
        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
                return err
        case <-ctx.Done():
                ks.startCancel()
                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
                        return nil
                }
                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %T", ks.Type)
        }
}' --new_str 'func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
                return "kind source: " + objectTypeString(ks.Type)
        }
        return "kind source: unknown type"
}

// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
// workers until the cache is synced.
func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
        select {
        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
                return err
        case <-ctx.Done():
                ks.startCancel()
                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
                        return nil
                }
                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", objectTypeString(ks.Type))
        }
}

// objectTypeString returns a human-readable string for the given object.
// For PartialObjectMetadata, it includes the underlying GVK so it'"'"'s easier to identify
// which resource a metadata-only watch is for.
func objectTypeString(obj runtime.Object) string {
        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        if poml, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); ok {
                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
}

func objectTypeStringForError(obj runtime.Object) string {
        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return gvk.String()
                }
        }
        if poml, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); ok {
                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return gvk.String()
                }
        }
        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
}'

    12	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    13	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
    14	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    15	        toolscache "k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
    16	        "k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue"
    17	        logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/internal/log"
    18	
    19	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache"
    20	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
    21	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler"
    22	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate"
    23	)
    24	
    25	var logKind = logf.RuntimeLog.WithName("source").WithName("Kind")
    26	
    27	// Kind is used to provide a source of events originating inside the cluster from Watches (e.g. Pod Create).
    28	type Kind[object client.Object, request comparable] struct {
    29	        // Type is the type of object to watch.  e.g. &v1.Pod{}
    30	        Type object
    31	
    32	        // Cache used to watch APIs
    33	        Cache cache.Cache
    34	
    35	        Handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request]
    36	
    37	        Predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
    38	
    39	        // startedErr may contain an error if one was encountered during startup. If its closed and does not
    40	        // contain an error, startup and syncing finished.
    41	        startedErr  chan error
    42	        startCancel func()
    43	}
    44	
    45	// Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to register an EventHandler with the Informer
    46	// to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
    47	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error {
    48	        if isNil(ks.Type) {
    49	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil object")
    50	        }
    51	        if isNil(ks.Cache) {
    52	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil cache")
    53	        }
    54	        if isNil(ks.Handler) {
    55	                return errors.New("must create Kind with non-nil handler")
    56	        }
    57	
    58	        // cache.GetInformer will block until its context is cancelled if the cache was already started and it can not
    59	        // sync that informer (most commonly due to RBAC issues).
    60	        ctx, ks.startCancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
    61	        ks.startedErr = make(chan error, 1) // Buffer chan to not leak goroutines if WaitForSync isn't called
    62	        go func() {
    63	                var (
    64	                        i       cache.Informer
    65	                        lastErr error
    66	                )
    67	
    68	                // Tries to get an informer until it returns true,
    69	                // an error or the specified context is cancelled or expired.
    70	                if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
    71	                        // Lookup the Informer from the Cache and add an EventHandler which populates the Queue
    72	                        i, lastErr = ks.Cache.GetInformer(ctx, ks.Type)
    73	                        if lastErr != nil {
    74	                                kindMatchErr := &meta.NoKindMatchError{}
    75	                                switch {
    76	                                case errors.As(lastErr, &kindMatchErr):
    77	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start",
    78	                                                "kind", kindMatchErr.GroupKind)
    79	                                case runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(lastErr):
    80	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "kind must be registered to the Scheme")
    81	                                default:
    82	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "failed to get informer from cache")
    83	                                }
    84	                                return false, nil // Retry.
    85	                        }
    86	                        return true, nil
    87	                }); err != nil {
    88	                        if lastErr != nil {
    89	                                ks.startedErr <- fmt.Errorf("failed to get informer from cache: %w", lastErr)
    90	                                return
    91	                        }
    92	                        ks.startedErr <- err
    93	                        return
    94	                }
    95	
    96	                handlerRegistration, err := i.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, ks.Handler, ks.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
    97	                        Logger: &logKind,
    98	                })
    99	                if err != nil {
   100	                        ks.startedErr <- err
   101	                        return
   102	                }
   103	                // First, wait for the cache to sync. For real caches this waits for startup.
   104	                // For fakes with Synced=false, this returns immediately allowing fast failure.
   105	                if !ks.Cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx) {
   106	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("cache did not sync")
   107	                        close(ks.startedErr)
   108	                        return
   109	                }
   110	                // Then wait for this specific handler to receive all initial events.
   111	                if !toolscache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), handlerRegistration.HasSynced) {
   112	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("handler did not sync")
   113	                }
   114	                close(ks.startedErr)
   115	        }()
   116	
   117	        return nil
   118	}
   119	
   120	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
   121	        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
   122	                return "kind source: " + objectTypeString(ks.Type)
   123	        }
   124	        return "kind source: unknown type"
   125	}
   126	
   127	// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
   128	// workers until the cache is synced.
   129	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
   130	        select {
   131	        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
   132	                return err
   133	        case <-ctx.Done():
   134	                ks.startCancel()
   135	                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
   136	                        return nil
   137	                }
   138	                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", objectTypeString(ks.Type))
   139	        }
   140	}
   141

   143	// For PartialObjectMetadata, it includes the underlying GVK so it's easier to identify
   144	// which resource a metadata-only watch is for.
   145	func objectTypeString(obj runtime.Object) string {
   146	        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
   147	                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
   148	                if gvk.Kind != "" {
   149	                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
   150	                }
   151	        }
   152	        if poml, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); ok {
   153	                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()
   154	                if gvk.Kind != "" {
   155	                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
   156	                }
   157	        }
   158	        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
   159	}

   162	        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
   163	                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()

   166	                }

   169	                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()

   171	                        return gvk.String()
   172	                }
   173	        }
   174	        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
   175	}

   177	func isNil(arg any) bool {
   178	        if v := reflect.ValueOf(arg); !v.IsValid() || ((v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr ||
   179	                v.Kind() == reflect.Interface ||
   180	                v.Kind() == reflect.Slice ||
   181	                v.Kind() == reflect.Map ||
   182	                v.Kind() == reflect.Chan ||
   183	                v.Kind() == reflect.Func) && v.IsNil()) {
   184	                return true
   185	        }
   186	        return false

// For PartialObjectMetadata, it includes the underlying GVK so it'"'"'s easier to identify
// which resource a metadata-only watch is for.
func objectTypeString(obj runtime.Object) string {
        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        if poml, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); ok {
                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
}

// For PartialObjectMetadata, it includes the underlying GVK so it'"'"'s easier to identify
// which resource a metadata-only watch is for.
func TypeString(obj runtime.Object) string {
        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }

                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
}

Now update the `String()` and `WaitForSync` methods to use `apiutil.TypeString`

str_replace_editor str_replace /testbed/pkg/internal/source/kind.go   --old_str 'func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
                return "kind source: " + objectTypeString(ks.Type)
        }
        return "kind source: unknown type"
}

// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
// workers until the cache is synced.
func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
        select {
        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
                return err
        case <-ctx.Done():
                ks.startCancel()
                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
                        return nil
                }
                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", objectTypeString(ks.Type))
        }
}

// objectTypeString returns a human-readable string for the given object.
// For PartialObjectMetadata, it includes the underlying GVK so it'"'"'s easier to identify
// which resource a metadata-only watch is for.
func objectTypeString(obj runtime.Object) string {
        if pom, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata); ok {
                gvk := pom.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        if poml, ok := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList); ok {
                gvk := poml.GroupVersionKind()
                if gvk.Kind != "" {
                        return fmt.Sprintf("%T (%s)", obj, gvk.String())
                }
        }
        return fmt.Sprintf("%T", obj)
}' --new_str 'func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
                return "kind source: " + apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type)
        }
        return "kind source: unknown type"
}

// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting

2026-05-30 01:11:22,748 - INFO - swea-agent-kubernetes-sigs__controller-runtime-3494 - 💭 THOUGHT
`apiutil` is already imported. Now let me update the `controller.go` as well. The controller.go line 355 uses `fmt.Sprintf("%T", watch)` as a fallback when the source doesn't implement `String()`. In this case, `watch` is a `source.TypedSource`, not a `runtime.Object`. So the `apiutil.TypeString` function wouldn't directly apply here. However, we could improve the logging for sources that wrap `Kind` source.

Actually, looking at the code:
```go
_, ok := watch.(interface {
    String() string
})
if !ok {
    log = log.WithValues("source", fmt.Sprintf("%T", watch))
} else {
    log = log.WithValues("source", fmt.Sprintf("%s", watch))
}
```

The `Kind` source implements `String()` via the method we just fixed. So this fallback path is for sources that don't implement `String()`. The `Kind` source already has `String()`, so it will go through the `else` branch and use our improved `String()` method. So this is fine.

But wait, there's also the `WaitForSync` error on line 381:
```go
err := fmt.Errorf("failed to wait for %s caches to sync %v: %w", c.Name, syncingSource, err)
```
Here `syncingSource` is a `source.TypedSyncingSource[request]` which should implement `String()`. So this should also use our improved `String()` method.

Now let me think about what else needs to be changed. The PR description shows these log lines:
```
"source":"kind source: *v1.PartialObjectMetadata"
"type":"*v1.PartialObjectMetadata"
```

The `"type":"*v1.PartialObjectMetadata"` comes from client-go's reflector (not controller-runtime). We can't change that directly. But the `"source":"kind source: *v1.PartialObjectMetadata"` comes from our `String()` method which we've now fixed.

Also the error message:
```
"err":"failed to wait for certificaterequest caches to sync kind source: *v1.PartialObjectMetadata: timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind *v1.PartialObjectMetadata"
```
This is a combination of our `String()` method and the `WaitForSync` error. Both should be fixed now.

Let me also check if there are any other places that use `%T` for objects that could be `PartialObjectMetadata`

🎬 ACTION

+	Predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
+
+	// startedErr may contain an error if one was encountered during startup. If its closed and does not
+	// contain an error, startup and syncing finished.
+	startedErr  chan error
+	startCancel func()
 }
 
 // Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to register an EventHandler with the Informer
 // to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
 func (ks *Kind[object, request]) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error {
-        if isNil(ks.Type) {
-                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil object")
-        }
-        if isNil(ks.Cache) {
-                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil cache")
-        }
-        if isNil(ks.Handler) {
-                return errors.New("must create Kind with non-nil handler")
-        }
-

-        // sync that informer (most commonly due to RBAC issues).
-        ctx, ks.startCancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)

-                var (
-                        i       cache.Informer
-                        lastErr error
-                )
-

-                // an error or the specified context is cancelled or expired.
-                if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
-                        // Lookup the Informer from the Cache and add an EventHandler which populates the Queue
-                        i, lastErr = ks.Cache.GetInformer(ctx, ks.Type)
-                        if lastErr != nil {
-                                kindMatchErr := &meta.NoKindMatchError{}
-                                switch {
-                                case errors.As(lastErr, &kindMatchErr):
-                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start",
-                                                "kind", kindMatchErr.GroupKind)
-                                case runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(lastErr):
-                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "kind must be registered to the Scheme")
-                                default:
-                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "failed to get informer from cache")
-                                }
-                                return false, nil // Retry.
-                        }
-                        return true, nil
-                }); err != nil {
-                        if lastErr != nil {
-                                ks.startedErr <- fmt.Errorf("failed to get informer from cache: %w", lastErr)
-                                return
-                        }
-                        ks.startedErr <- err
-                        return
-                }
-
-                handlerRegistration, err := i.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, ks.Handler, ks.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
-                        Logger: &logKind,
-                })
-                if err != nil {
-                        ks.startedErr <- err
-                        return
-                }
-                // First, wait for the cache to sync. For real caches this waits for startup.
-                // For fakes with Synced=false, this returns immediately allowing fast failure.
-                if !ks.Cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx) {
-                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("cache did not sync")
-                        close(ks.startedErr)
-                        return
-                }
-                // Then wait for this specific handler to receive all initial events.
-                if !toolscache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), handlerRegistration.HasSynced) {
-                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("handler did not sync")
-                }
-                close(ks.startedErr)
-        }()
-
-        return nil
+	if isNil(ks.Type) {
+		return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil object")
+	}
+	if isNil(ks.Cache) {
+		return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil cache")
+	}
+	if isNil(ks.Handler) {
+		return errors.New("must create Kind with non-nil handler")
+	}
+
+	// cache.GetInformer will block until its context is cancelled if the cache was already started and it can not
+	// sync that informer (most commonly due to RBAC issues).
+	ctx, ks.startCancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
+	ks.startedErr = make(chan error, 1) // Buffer chan to not leak goroutines if WaitForSync isn't called
+	go func() {
+		var (
+			i       cache.Informer
+			lastErr error
+		)
+
+		// Tries to get an informer until it returns true,
+		// an error or the specified context is cancelled or expired.
+		if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
+			// Lookup the Informer from the Cache and add an EventHandler which populates the Queue
+			i, lastErr = ks.Cache.GetInformer(ctx, ks.Type)
+			if lastErr != nil {
+				kindMatchErr := &meta.NoKindMatchError{}
+				switch {
+				case errors.As(lastErr, &kindMatchErr):
+					logKind.Error(lastErr, "if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start",
+						"kind", kindMatchErr.GroupKind)
+				case runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(lastErr):
+					logKind.Error(lastErr, "kind must be registered to the Scheme")
+				default:
+					logKind.Error(lastErr, "failed to get informer from cache")
+				}
+				return false, nil // Retry.
+			}
+			return true, nil
+		}); err != nil {
+			if lastErr != nil {
+				ks.startedErr <- fmt.Errorf("failed to get informer from cache: %w", lastErr)
+				return
+			}
+			ks.startedErr <- err
+			return
+		}
+
+		handlerRegistration, err := i.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, ks.Handler, ks.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
+			Logger: &logKind,
+		})
+		if err != nil {
+			ks.startedErr <- err
+			return
+		}
+		// First, wait for the cache to sync. For real caches this waits for startup.
+		// For fakes with Synced=false, this returns immediately allowing fast failure.
+		if !ks.Cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx) {
+			ks.startedErr <- errors.New("cache did not sync")
+			close(ks.startedErr)
+			return
+		}
+		// Then wait for this specific handler to receive all initial events.
+		if !toolscache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), handlerRegistration.HasSynced) {
+			ks.startedErr <- errors.New("handler did not sync")
+		}
+		close(ks.startedErr)
+	}()
+
+	return nil
 }
 
 func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
-        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
-                return "kind source: " + apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type)
-        }
-        return "kind source: unknown type"
+	if !isNil(ks.Type) {
+		return "kind source: " + apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type)
+	}
+	return "kind source: unknown type"
 }
 
 // WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
 // workers until the cache is synced.
 func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
-        select {
-        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
-                return err
-        case <-ctx.Done():
-                ks.startCancel()
-                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
-                        return nil
-                }
-                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type))
-        }
+	select {
+	case err := <-ks.startedErr:
+		return err
+	case <-ctx.Done():
+		ks.startCancel()
+		if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
+			return nil
+		}
+		return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type))
+	}
 }
 
 func isNil(arg any) bool {
-        if v := reflect.ValueOf(arg); !v.IsValid() || ((v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr ||
-                v.Kind() == reflect.Interface ||
-                v.Kind() == reflect.Slice ||
-                v.Kind() == reflect.Map ||
-                v.Kind() == reflect.Chan ||
-                v.Kind() == reflect.Func) && v.IsNil()) {
-                return true
-        }
-        return false
+	if v := reflect.ValueOf(arg); !v.IsValid() || ((v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr ||
+		v.Kind() == reflect.Interface ||
+		v.Kind() == reflect.Slice ||
+		v.Kind() == reflect.Map ||
+		v.Kind() == reflect.Chan ||
+		v.Kind() == reflect.Func) && v.IsNil()) {
+		return true
+	}
+	return false
 }

+	// will be populated using the cached Group if available. This can lead to failures updating
+	// the cache with new Versions of CRDs registered at runtime.

 // GVKForObject finds the GroupVersionKind associated with the given object, if there is only a single such GVK.
 func GVKForObject(obj runtime.Object, scheme *runtime.Scheme) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {
-        // TODO(directxman12): do we want to generalize this to arbitrary container types?
-        // I think we'd need a generalized form of scheme or something.  It's a
-        // shame there's not a reliable "GetGVK" interface that works by default
-        // for unpopulated static types and populated "dynamic" types

-        _, isPartialList := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList)

-                gvk := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
-                if len(gvk.Kind) == 0 {
-                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingKindErr("unstructured object has no kind")
-                }
-                if len(gvk.Version) == 0 {
-                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingVersionErr("unstructured object has no version")
-                }
-                return gvk, nil
-        }
-
-        // Use the given scheme to retrieve all the GVKs for the object.
-        gvks, isUnversioned, err := scheme.ObjectKinds(obj)
-        if err != nil {
-                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err
-        }
-        if isUnversioned {
-                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("cannot create group-version-kind for unversioned type %T", obj)
-        }
-
-        switch {
-        case len(gvks) < 1:
-                // If the object has no GVK, the object might not have been registered with the scheme.
-                // or it's not a valid object.
-                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("no GroupVersionKind associated with Go type %T, was the type registered with the Scheme?", obj)
-        case len(gvks) > 1:
-                err := fmt.Errorf("multiple GroupVersionKinds associated with Go type %T within the Scheme, this can happen when a type is registered for multiple GVKs at the same time", obj)
-

-                currentGVK := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()

-                                if gvk == currentGVK {
-                                        return gvk, nil
-                                }
-                        }
-
-                        return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
-                                "%w: the object's supplied GroupVersionKind %q was not found in the Scheme's list; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, currentGVK, gvks)
-                }
-
-                // This should only trigger for things like metav1.XYZ --
-                // normal versioned types should be fine.
-                //
-                // See https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/362
-                // for more information.
-                return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
-                        "%w: callers can either fix their type registration to only register it once, or specify the GroupVersionKind to use for object passed in; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, gvks)
-        default:
-                // In any other case, we've found a single GVK for the object.
-                return gvks[0], nil
-        }
+	// TODO(directxman12): do we want to generalize this to arbitrary container types?
+	// I think we'd need a generalized form of scheme or something.  It's a
+	// shame there's not a reliable "GetGVK" interface that works by default
+	// for unpopulated static types and populated "dynamic" types
+	// (unstructured, partial, etc)
+
+	// check for PartialObjectMetadata, which is analogous to unstructured, but isn't handled by ObjectKinds
+	_, isPartial := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadata)
+	_, isPartialList := obj.(*metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList)
+	if isPartial || isPartialList {
+		// we require that the GVK be populated in order to recognize the object
+		gvk := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
+		if len(gvk.Kind) == 0 {
+			return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingKindErr("unstructured object has no kind")
+		}
+		if len(gvk.Version) == 0 {
+			return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, runtime.NewMissingVersionErr("unstructured object has no version")
+		}
+		return gvk, nil
+	}
+
+	// Use the given scheme to retrieve all the GVKs for the object.
+	gvks, isUnversioned, err := scheme.ObjectKinds(obj)
+	if err != nil {
+		return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err
+	}
+	if isUnversioned {
+		return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("cannot create group-version-kind for unversioned type %T", obj)
+	}
+
+	switch {
+	case len(gvks) < 1:
+		// If the object has no GVK, the object might not have been registered with the scheme.
+		// or it's not a valid object.
+		return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf("no GroupVersionKind associated with Go type %T, was the type registered with the Scheme?", obj)
+	case len(gvks) > 1:
+		err := fmt.Errorf("multiple GroupVersionKinds associated with Go type %T within the Scheme, this can happen when a type is registered for multiple GVKs at the same time", obj)
+
+		// We've found multiple GVKs for the object.
+		currentGVK := obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()
+		if !currentGVK.Empty() {
+			// If the base object has a GVK, check if it's in the list of GVKs before using it.
+			for _, gvk := range gvks {
+				if gvk == currentGVK {
+					return gvk, nil
+				}
+			}
+
+			return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
+				"%w: the object's supplied GroupVersionKind %q was not found in the Scheme's list; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, currentGVK, gvks)
+		}
+
+		// This should only trigger for things like metav1.XYZ --
+		// normal versioned types should be fine.
+		//
+		// See https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/362
+		// for more information.
+		return schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(
+			"%w: callers can either fix their type registration to only register it once, or specify the GroupVersionKind to use for object passed in; refusing to guess at one: %q", err, gvks)
+	default:
+		// In any other case, we've found a single GVK for the object.
+		return gvks[0], nil
+	}
 }

-        ip.mu.Lock()

-        return nil
+	if err := func() error {
+		ip.mu.Lock()
+		defer ip.mu.Unlock()
+
+		if ip.started {
+			return errors.New("informer already started") //nolint:stylecheck
+		}
+
+		// Set the context so it can be passed to informers that are added later
+		ip.ctx = ctx
+
+		// Start each informer
+		for _, i := range ip.tracker.Structured {
+			ip.startInformerLocked(i)
+		}
+		for _, i := range ip.tracker.Unstructured {
+			ip.startInformerLocked(i)
+		}
+		for _, i := range ip.tracker.Metadata {
+			ip.startInformerLocked(i)
+		}
+
+		// Set started to true so we immediately start any informers added later.

+

+	}(); err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+	<-ctx.Done() // Block until the context is done
+	ip.mu.Lock()
+	ip.stopped = true // Set stopped to true so we don't start any new informers
+	ip.mu.Unlock()
+	ip.waitGroup.Wait() // Block until all informers have stopped
+	return nil
 }

+	// Don't start the informer in case we are already waiting for the items in
+	// the waitGroup to finish, since waitGroups don't support waiting and adding
+	// at the same time.
+	if ip.stopped {
+		return
+	}
+
+	ip.waitGroup.Go(func() {

 // Get will create a new Informer and add it to the map of specificInformersMap if none exists. Returns
 // the Informer from the map.

-        i, started, ok := ip.Peek(gvk, obj)

-                        return false, nil, &ErrResourceNotCached{GVK: gvk}
-                }
-                var err error
-                if i, started, err = ip.addInformerToMap(gvk, obj); err != nil {

-                }

-                shouldBlock = *opts.BlockUntilSynced
-        }
-

-                // Wait for it to sync before returning the Informer so that folks don't read from a stale cache.
-                if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), i.Informer.HasSynced) {
-                        return started, nil, apierrors.NewTimeoutError(fmt.Sprintf("failed waiting for %s Informer to sync", apiutil.TypeString(obj)), 0)
-                }

+	// Return the informer if it is found
+	i, started, ok := ip.Peek(gvk, obj)
+	if !ok {
+		if readerFailOnMissingInformer {
+			return false, nil, &ErrResourceNotCached{GVK: gvk}
+		}
+		var err error
+		if i, started, err = ip.addInformerToMap(gvk, obj); err != nil {
+			return started, nil, err
+		}
+	}
+
+	shouldBlock := true
+	if opts.BlockUntilSynced != nil {
+		shouldBlock = *opts.BlockUntilSynced
+	}
+
+	if shouldBlock && started && !i.Informer.HasSynced() {
+		// Wait for it to sync before returning the Informer so that folks don't read from a stale cache.
+		if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), i.Informer.HasSynced) {
+			return started, nil, apierrors.NewTimeoutError(fmt.Sprintf("failed waiting for %s Informer to sync", apiutil.TypeString(obj)), 0)
+		}
+	}
+
+	return started, i, nil
 }

-        if ip.watchErrorHandler != nil {
-                if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext(ip.watchErrorHandler); err != nil {
-                        return nil, false, err
-                }
-        }
-
-        // Check to see if there is a transformer for this gvk
-        if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetTransform(ip.transform); err != nil {
-                return nil, false, err
-        }
-
-        mapping, err := ip.mapper.RESTMapping(gvk.GroupKind(), gvk.Version)
-        if err != nil {
-                return nil, false, err
-        }
-
-        // Create the new entry and set it in the map.
-        i := &Cache{
-                Informer: sharedIndexInformer,
-                Reader: CacheReader{
-                        indexer:          sharedIndexInformer.GetIndexer(),
-                        groupVersionKind: gvk,
-                        scopeName:        mapping.Scope.Name(),
-                        disableDeepCopy:  ip.unsafeDisableDeepCopy,
-                },
-                stop: make(chan struct{}),
-        }
-        ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk] = i
-
-        // Start the informer in case the InformersMap has started, otherwise it will be
-        // started when the InformersMap starts.
-        if ip.started {
-                ip.startInformerLocked(i)
-        }
-        return i, ip.started, nil
+	ip.mu.Lock()
+	defer ip.mu.Unlock()
+
+	// Check the cache to see if we already have an Informer. If we do, return the Informer.
+	// This is for the case where 2 routines tried to get the informer when it wasn't in the map
+	// so neither returned early, but the first one created it.
+	if i, ok := ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk]; ok {
+		return i, ip.started, nil
+	}
+
+	// Create a NewSharedIndexInformer and add it to the map.
+	listWatcher, err := ip.makeListWatcher(gvk, obj)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, false, err
+	}
+	sharedIndexInformer := ip.newInformer(&cache.ListWatch{
+		ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
+			ip.selector.ApplyToList(&opts)
+			return listWatcher.ListWithContextFunc(ctx, opts)
+		},
+		WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
+			opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
+			opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
+
+			ip.selector.ApplyToList(&opts)
+			return listWatcher.WatchFuncWithContext(ctx, opts)
+		},
+	}, obj, calculateResyncPeriod(ip.resync), cache.Indexers{
+		cache.NamespaceIndex: cache.MetaNamespaceIndexFunc,
+	})
+
+	// Set WatchErrorHandler on SharedIndexInformer if set
+	if ip.watchErrorHandler != nil {
+		if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext(ip.watchErrorHandler); err != nil {
+			return nil, false, err
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Check to see if there is a transformer for this gvk
+	if err := sharedIndexInformer.SetTransform(ip.transform); err != nil {
+		return nil, false, err
+	}
+
+	mapping, err := ip.mapper.RESTMapping(gvk.GroupKind(), gvk.Version)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, false, err
+	}
+
+	// Create the new entry and set it in the map.
+	i := &Cache{
+		Informer: sharedIndexInformer,
+		Reader: CacheReader{
+			indexer:          sharedIndexInformer.GetIndexer(),
+			groupVersionKind: gvk,
+			scopeName:        mapping.Scope.Name(),
+			disableDeepCopy:  ip.unsafeDisableDeepCopy,
+		},
+		stop: make(chan struct{}),
+	}
+	ip.informersByType(obj)[gvk] = i
+
+	// Start the informer in case the InformersMap has started, otherwise it will be
+	// started when the InformersMap starts.
+	if ip.started {
+		ip.startInformerLocked(i)
+	}
+	return i, ip.started, nil
 }

+	// Kubernetes APIs work against Resources, not GroupVersionKinds.  Map the
+	// groupVersionKind to the Resource API we will use.
+	mapping, err := ip.mapper.RESTMapping(gvk.GroupKind(), gvk.Version)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	// Figure out if the GVK we're dealing with is global, or namespace scoped.
+	var namespace string
+	if mapping.Scope.Name() == meta.RESTScopeNameNamespace {
+		namespace = restrictNamespaceBySelector(ip.namespace, ip.selector)
+	}
+
+	switch obj.(type) {
+	//
+	// Unstructured
+	//
+	case runtime.Unstructured:
+		// If the rest configuration has a negotiated serializer passed in,
+		// we should remove it and use the one that the dynamic client sets for us.
+		cfg := rest.CopyConfig(ip.config)
+		cfg.NegotiatedSerializer = nil
+		dynamicClient, err := dynamic.NewForConfigAndClient(cfg, ip.httpClient)
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, err
+		}
+		resources := dynamicClient.Resource(mapping.Resource)
+		return &cache.ListWatch{
+			ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
+				if namespace != "" {
+					return resources.Namespace(namespace).List(ctx, opts)
+				}
+				return resources.List(ctx, opts)
+			},
+			// Setup the watch function
+			WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
+				opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
+				opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
+
+				if namespace != "" {
+					return resources.Namespace(namespace).Watch(ctx, opts)
+				}
+				return resources.Watch(ctx, opts)
+			},
+		}, nil
+	//
+	// Metadata
+	//
+	case *metav1.PartialObjectMetadata, *metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList:
+		// Always clear the negotiated serializer and use the one
+		// set from the metadata client.
+		cfg := rest.CopyConfig(ip.config)
+		cfg.NegotiatedSerializer = nil
+
+		// Grab the metadata metadataClient.
+		metadataClient, err := metadata.NewForConfigAndClient(cfg, ip.httpClient)
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, err
+		}
+		resources := metadataClient.Resource(mapping.Resource)
+
+		return &cache.ListWatch{
+			ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
+				var (
+					list *metav1.PartialObjectMetadataList
+					err  error
+				)
+				if namespace != "" {
+					list, err = resources.Namespace(namespace).List(ctx, opts)
+				} else {
+					list, err = resources.List(ctx, opts)
+				}
+				if list != nil {
+					for i := range list.Items {
+						list.Items[i].SetGroupVersionKind(gvk)
+					}
+				}
+				return list, err
+			},
+			// Setup the watch function
+			WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watcher watch.Interface, err error) {
+				opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
+				opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
+
+				if namespace != "" {
+					watcher, err = resources.Namespace(namespace).Watch(ctx, opts)
+				} else {
+					watcher, err = resources.Watch(ctx, opts)
+				}
+				if err != nil {
+					return nil, err
+				}
+				return newGVKFixupWatcher(gvk, watcher), nil
+			},
+		}, nil
+	//
+	// Structured.
+	//
+	default:
+		client, err := apiutil.RESTClientForGVK(gvk, false, false, ip.config, ip.codecs, ip.httpClient)
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, err
+		}
+		listGVK := gvk.GroupVersion().WithKind(gvk.Kind + "List")
+		listObj, err := ip.scheme.New(listGVK)
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, err
+		}
+		return &cache.ListWatch{
+			ListWithContextFunc: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {
+				// Build the request.
+				req := client.Get().Resource(mapping.Resource.Resource).VersionedParams(&opts, ip.paramCodec)
+				if namespace != "" {
+					req.Namespace(namespace)
+				}
+
+				// Create the resulting object, and execute the request.
+				res := listObj.DeepCopyObject()
+				if err := req.Do(ctx).Into(res); err != nil {
+					return nil, err
+				}
+				return res, nil
+			},
+			// Setup the watch function
+			WatchFuncWithContext: func(ctx context.Context, opts metav1.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
+				opts.Watch = true // Watch needs to be set to true separately
+				opts.AllowWatchBookmarks = ip.enableWatchBookmarks
+
+				// Build the request.
+				req := client.Get().Resource(mapping.Resource.Resource).VersionedParams(&opts, ip.paramCodec)
+				if namespace != "" {
+					req.Namespace(namespace)
+				}
+				// Call the watch.
+				return req.Watch(ctx)
+			},
+		}, nil
+	}
 }

 // watch.Filter which is compatible.

 func newGVKFixupWatcher(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, watcher watch.Interface) watch.Interface {
-        return watch.Filter(

 // this is so that multiple controllers don't get into lock-step and all
 // hammer the apiserver with list requests simultaneously.
 func calculateResyncPeriod(resync time.Duration) time.Duration {

-        factor := rand.Float64()/5.0 + 0.9

+	factor := rand.Float64()/5.0 + 0.9
+	return time.Duration(float64(resync.Nanoseconds()) * factor)
 }

 // if not default/empty, or the namespace that a ListWatch for the specific resource

 func restrictNamespaceBySelector(namespaceOpt string, s Selector) string {

-                // namespace is already restricted
-                return namespaceOpt

+		return namespaceOpt
+	}
+	fieldSelector := s.Field
+	if fieldSelector == nil || fieldSelector.Empty() {
+		return ""
+	}
+	// check whether a selector includes the namespace field
+	value, found := fieldSelector.RequiresExactMatch("metadata.namespace")
+	if found {
+		return value
+	}
+	return ""
 }

     1	package internal
     2	
     3	import (
     4	        "context"
     5	        "errors"
     6	        "fmt"
     7	        "reflect"
     8	        "time"
     9	
    10	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
    11	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    12	        "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    13	        toolscache "k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
    14	        "k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue"
    15	        logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/internal/log"
    16	
    17	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache"
    18	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
    19	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client/apiutil"
    20	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler"
    21	        "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate"
    22	)
    23	
    24	var logKind = logf.RuntimeLog.WithName("source").WithName("Kind")
    25	
    26	// Kind is used to provide a source of events originating inside the cluster from Watches (e.g. Pod Create).
    27	type Kind[object client.Object, request comparable] struct {
    28	        // Type is the type of object to watch.  e.g. &v1.Pod{}
    29	        Type object
    30	
    31	        // Cache used to watch APIs
    32	        Cache cache.Cache
    33	
    34	        Handler handler.TypedEventHandler[object, request]
    35	
    36	        Predicates []predicate.TypedPredicate[object]
    37	
    38	        // startedErr may contain an error if one was encountered during startup. If its closed and does not
    39	        // contain an error, startup and syncing finished.
    40	        startedErr  chan error
    41	        startCancel func()
    42	}
    43	
    44	// Start is internal and should be called only by the Controller to register an EventHandler with the Informer
    45	// to enqueue reconcile.Requests.
    46	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) Start(ctx context.Context, queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[request]) error {
    47	        if isNil(ks.Type) {
    48	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil object")
    49	        }
    50	        if isNil(ks.Cache) {
    51	                return fmt.Errorf("must create Kind with a non-nil cache")
    52	        }
    53	        if isNil(ks.Handler) {
    54	                return errors.New("must create Kind with non-nil handler")
    55	        }
    56	
    57	        // cache.GetInformer will block until its context is cancelled if the cache was already started and it can not
    58	        // sync that informer (most commonly due to RBAC issues).
    59	        ctx, ks.startCancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
    60	        ks.startedErr = make(chan error, 1) // Buffer chan to not leak goroutines if WaitForSync isn't called
    61	        go func() {
    62	                var (
    63	                        i       cache.Informer
    64	                        lastErr error
    65	                )
    66	
    67	                // Tries to get an informer until it returns true,
    68	                // an error or the specified context is cancelled or expired.
    69	                if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
    70	                        // Lookup the Informer from the Cache and add an EventHandler which populates the Queue
    71	                        i, lastErr = ks.Cache.GetInformer(ctx, ks.Type)
    72	                        if lastErr != nil {
    73	                                kindMatchErr := &meta.NoKindMatchError{}
    74	                                switch {
    75	                                case errors.As(lastErr, &kindMatchErr):
    76	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start",
    77	                                                "kind", kindMatchErr.GroupKind)
    78	                                case runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(lastErr):
    79	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "kind must be registered to the Scheme")
    80	                                default:
    81	                                        logKind.Error(lastErr, "failed to get informer from cache")
    82	                                }
    83	                                return false, nil // Retry.
    84	                        }
    85	                        return true, nil
    86	                }); err != nil {
    87	                        if lastErr != nil {
    88	                                ks.startedErr <- fmt.Errorf("failed to get informer from cache: %w", lastErr)
    89	                                return
    90	                        }
    91	                        ks.startedErr <- err
    92	                        return
    93	                }
    94	
    95	                handlerRegistration, err := i.AddEventHandlerWithOptions(NewEventHandler(ctx, queue, ks.Handler, ks.Predicates), toolscache.HandlerOptions{
    96	                        Logger: &logKind,
    97	                })
    98	                if err != nil {
    99	                        ks.startedErr <- err
   100	                        return
   101	                }
   102	                // First, wait for the cache to sync. For real caches this waits for startup.
   103	                // For fakes with Synced=false, this returns immediately allowing fast failure.
   104	                if !ks.Cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx) {
   105	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("cache did not sync")
   106	                        close(ks.startedErr)
   107	                        return
   108	                }
   109	                // Then wait for this specific handler to receive all initial events.
   110	                if !toolscache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), handlerRegistration.HasSynced) {
   111	                        ks.startedErr <- errors.New("handler did not sync")
   112	                }
   113	                close(ks.startedErr)
   114	        }()
   115	
   116	        return nil
   117	}
   118	
   119	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) String() string {
   120	        if !isNil(ks.Type) {
   121	                return "kind source: " + apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type)
   122	        }
   123	        return "kind source: unknown type"
   124	}
   125	
   126	// WaitForSync implements SyncingSource to allow controllers to wait with starting
   127	// workers until the cache is synced.
   128	func (ks *Kind[object, request]) WaitForSync(ctx context.Context) error {
   129	        select {
   130	        case err := <-ks.startedErr:
   131	                return err
   132	        case <-ctx.Done():
   133	                ks.startCancel()
   134	                if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) {
   135	                        return nil
   136	                }
   137	                return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for cache to be synced for Kind %s", apiutil.TypeString(ks.Type))
   138	        }
   139	}
   140	
   141	func isNil(arg any) bool {
   142	        if v := reflect.ValueOf(arg); !v.IsValid() || ((v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr ||
   143	                v.Kind() == reflect.Interface ||
   144	                v.Kind() == reflect.Slice ||
   145	                v.Kind() == reflect.Map ||
   146	                v.Kind() == reflect.Chan ||
   147	                v.Kind() == reflect.Func) && v.IsNil()) {
   148	                return true
   149	        }
   150	        return false
   151	}
   152
